# GameZipper 100% Master Test Case Library
**Version**: 1.78.0
**Effective Date**: 2026-06-20
**Build**: R198 (Dynamic Test Intelligence 4h cron evolution)
**Library Owner**: Lead Test Engineer
**Version Note**: v1.78.0 = R198 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 8 new test cases: CVE-2026-34621 Adobe Acrobat / Acrobat Reader zero-day (CVSS 8.6) — exploit code appeared Nov 2025 in the wild, Adobe shipped fix mid-2026 after a 4+ month disclosure-lag window; PDF-as-malware-vector where players who open a "game manual PDF" or "newsletter PDF" attached to a malicious Monetag/AdSense creative get fully compromised — the PDF-MEDIA-VECTOR-DELAYED-DISCLOSURE-LONG-WINDOW dimension distinct from S-121 (npm Ghost campaign fake-install-log) because S-152 is the END-USER-SIDE PDF dimension not the developer-side npm dimension (ithome.com/tag/lingriloudong/ IT之家 zero-day tracker); CVE-2026-21962 Oracle WebLogic Server Proxy Plug-in unauthenticated RCE (June 10 2026) — security-check-before-URL-decode ordering flaw → complete auth bypass + RCE + backend cluster penetration — the WEB-SERVER-PROXY-PLUGIN-UNAUTH-RCE-ORDERING-FLAW dimension distinct from W-163 (CVE-2026-21992 Oracle Identity Manager RCE — different Oracle product) and S-069 (HTTP/2 Rapid Reset DoS — different vuln class) because S-153 is the PROXY-PLUGIN-ORDERING-FLAW dimension (blog.csdn.net/2302_79436907/article/details/159896100 June 10 2026); CVE-2026-23918 Apache HTTP/2 mod_http2 Double-Free (May 6 2026, single-version 2.4.66 only) — unauthenticated HEADERS + RST_STREAM frame pair → double-free → potential RCE — the WEB-SERVER-HTTP2-DOUBLE-FREE-LIBRARY-LEVEL dimension distinct from S-069 (HTTP/2 Rapid Reset DoS — different protocol-level vuln class) and S-086 (NGINX heap overflow — NGINX-specific not Apache) because S-154 is the APACHE-mod_http2-LIBRARY-LEVEL dimension (blog.csdn.net/weixin_42376192/article/details/161003983 May 12 2026); CVE-2026-34926 Trend Micro Apex One path traversal + EDR-fleet hijack (May 21 2026 GHSA-4ccp-cqrh-3w9v, CISA KEV May 21 2026, federal fix deadline June 4 2026) — pre-authenticated local attacker modifies key table → injects malicious code into auto-update channel → silently pushes malware to entire EDR-protected endpoint fleet — the ENDPOINT-PROTECTION-PLATFORM-COMPROMISE-VIA-AUTO-UPDATE-CHANNEL dimension distinct from S-074 (CSP tightening defense-side regression) and S-118 (DoubleClick RAT malspam ad-platform abuse) because S-155 is the SECURITY-PRODUCT-BECOMES-MALWARE-DISTRIBUTION dimension — the endpoint protection platform is itself turned into the attacker's distribution channel (github.com/advisories/GHSA-4ccp-cqrh-3w9v May 21 2026; blog.csdn.net/weixin_42376192/article/details/161357281 May 24 2026); CVE-2026-34714 Vim modeline default-config arbitrary command execution (CVSS 9.2) — tabpanel option missing P_MLE flag allows %{expr} injection WITHOUT enabling modelineexpr; autocmd_add() missing check_secure() bypasses sandbox → arbitrary OS command execution when victim opens a malicious file — the DEVELOPER-TOOL-DEFAULT-CONFIG-RCE dimension distinct from C-109 (Apifox desktop-client CDN poisoning — network-side dev-tool attack) and S-101 (AI extension — browser extension) because S-156 is the EDITOR-DEFAULT-CONFIG-MODELINE-EXEC dimension affecting any GameZipper dev who opens a seemingly-harmless text file (blog.csdn.net/ximenjianxue/article/details/160021682 April 15 2026); GhostClaw / @openclaw-ai/openclawai npm scoped-namespace typosquat (eSecurity Planet March 10 2026) — a malicious npm package disguised as the OpenClaw installer, multi-stage infostealer + RAT stealing developer credentials, cryptocurrency wallets, and AI-CLI tokens — the AI-CLI-SCOPED-NPM-TYPOSQUAT-VIA-NAMESPACE-IMPERSONATION dimension distinct from S-128 (OpenClaw ClawHub marketplace malware — INSIDE the official marketplace) and S-129 (ClickFix macOS AppleScript delivery — social engineering) because S-157 is the SCOPED-NPM-NAMESPACE-TYPOSQUAT dimension where the attacker registers a deceptive scoped name (@openclaw-ai/) rather than the legitimate (@openclaw/) — a NEW vector in the AI-CLI impersonation family (esecurityplanet.com/threats/fake-openclaw-npm-package-installs-ghostclaw-malware March 10 2026); CVE-2026-26176 Windows csc.sys Client-Side Caching driver TOCTOU privilege escalation (April 2026) — CscQueryDirectory function race condition (TOCTOU) allows unprivileged user → SYSTEM privilege escalation on Windows 11 26H2 — the WINDOWS-DRIVER-TOCTOU-LPE dimension distinct from B-080 (CVE-2026-32202 Windows Shell spoofing CISA KEV — Shell UI not kernel-driver) and B-044 (Android June 2026 124-CVE bulletin — different OS) because B-115 is the OS-INTERNAL-DRIVER-LPE affecting Windows machines hosting browsers used by GameZipper players (blog.csdn.net/oShuangYue12/article/details/160360940 June 16 2026); CVE-2026-31431 "Copy Fail" Linux AF_ALG / splice() LPE (May 7 2026 Xint Code) — a 732-byte Python PoC achieves 100% reliable local privilege escalation on ALL Linux distributions with kernels built 2017 → patch date via a single 4-byte page-cache write chained through the authencesn logic flaw in AF_ALG + splice() — the BROAD-SCOPE-LINUX-LPE-AFFECTS-HOSTING-INFRA dimension distinct from B-115 (Windows-driver-LPE — different OS) and S-153 (WebLogic RCE — server-app-layer not kernel-layer) because P-074 is the KERNEL-LAYER-LPE dimension affecting the Linux hosting/CDN infrastructure serving GameZipper (blog.csdn.net/safetybug/article/details/160644342 May 7 2026; xint.io/blog/copy-fail-linux-distributions May 7 2026).
**Version Note**: v1.77.0 = R197 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 7 new test cases: CVE-2026-28962 Safari 26.5 WebKit CSP-bypass information disclosure (May 14 2026 Apple security list, tracked as WebKit fix for "绕过内容安全策略,泄露用户敏感信息") — the WEBKIT-CSP-BYPASS-INFORMATION-DISCLOSURE dimension distinct from S-138 (Safari 26.5 WebRTC process-crash fix — the WebRTC subsystem fix from the SAME 26.5 advisory) because S-150 is the CONTENT-SECURITY-POLICY dimension where malicious page content can circumvent the page-declared CSP and exfiltrate data the CSP should have blocked (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_0236a054d4370952 May 14 2026 IT之家 + Apple Safari 26.5 security list); CVE-2026-26171 Microsoft .NET System.Security.Cryptography.Xml EncryptedXml uncontrolled-resource-consumption DoS (April 21 2026 Microsoft Security Advisory via PowerShell/Announcements issue #83 — affects PowerShell 7 < 7.6.1 and < 7.5.6; EncryptedXml class can be driven by attacker-crafted XML into unbounded resource consumption) — the XML-CRYPTOGRAPHY-LIBRARY-DoS dimension distinct from prior CVE entries because the EncryptedXml class is the XML-encryption primitive used in SAML/WS-Federation/SOAP-message-security/WS-Trust stacks — any future GameZipper backend or third-party-ad-server-side .NET component that decrypts XML-bearing payloads inherits the DoS (github.com/PowerShell/Announcements/issues/83 April 21 2026); WebKit Interop 2026 — 20 OFFICIAL cross-browser focus areas announced Feb 13 2026 by Apple WebKit (anchor positioning, advanced attr(), container style queries, contrast-color(), custom highlight, dialog/popover improvements, fetch upload + range requests, getAllRecords() for IndexedDB, JSPI for Wasm, media pseudo-classes, Navigation API, scoped custom-element registries, scroll-driven animations, scroll snapping, shape(), view transitions, web compat, WebRTC, WebTransport, CSS zoom) — the OFFICIAL-MULTI-VENDOR-CROSS-BROWSER-COMPATIBILITY-BASELINE-2026 dimension distinct from W-150 (WASM 3.0 single language features), W-154 (Chrome 150 HTML-in-Canvas single browser), and C-118 (Safari 27 58 features single vendor) because W-181 is the MULTI-VENDOR commitment that EVERY Chrome/Safari/Firefox release in 2026 must converge on — defining the 20 features whose cross-browser parity GameZipper games can rely on (new.qq.com/rain/a/20260213A015ZI00 Feb 13 2026 + WebKit blog); iOS 27 device compatibility drop to iPhone 12+ (Macworld June 4 2026 + WWDC26) — iPhone 11 / XR / XS and earlier are STUCK on iOS 26 indefinitely, meaning ~30%+ of iOS Safari traffic will run on STALE WebKit for years — the DEVICE-DROP-LONG-TAILED-STALE-WEBKIT-SURFACE dimension distinct from P-071 (12GB-RAM hardware gating for AI features) and W-155 (iOS 27 stable ship full-stack regression) because B-114 is the LONG-LIVED dimension where iPhone-11-and-earlier users never receive iOS 27+ patches, so any iOS 26 WebKit CVE remains exploitable on that sub-population for the device's lifetime (macworld.com/article/1659017/ios-versions-list.html June 4 2026 + Macworld WWDC26 device-compatibility list); Apple ImageCreator class deprecation in iOS 27 / iPadOS 27 / macOS 27 / visionOS 27 (June 11 2026 Apple Developer news) — apps using ImageCreator (the original Image Playground framework entrypoint) will: in beta OS releases emit Xcode warnings + cause runtime errors in TestFlight builds; in public OS releases the code WILL NOT COMPILE — the FRAMEWORK-API-REMOVAL-SILENT-BREAKING-CHANGE dimension distinct from any prior API-deprecation test case because Apple is removing a PUBLIC API that previously shipped with iOS 26 with NO runtime shim — any future GameZipper iOS app or PWA wrapper that uses Image Playground via ImageCreator will silently break on iOS 27 stable ship (developer.apple.com/news/?id=08242015a June 11 2026); WebGPU vs WebGL client-side AI inference benchmarks (SitePoint Feb 25 2026 — "WebGPU vs. WebGL: Performance Benchmarks for Client-Side Inference") — running LLMs and vision models directly in the browser has shifted from novelty to genuine engineering goal; the WebGPU-vs-WebGL CLIENT-SIDE-AI-INFERENCE dimension distinct from P-067 (WebGPU+WASM shared-memory boundary — graphics/compute) and P-069 (WebGPU 39x draw-call throughput — pure-graphics metric) because P-073 is the AI-INFERENCE dimension — relevant to any GameZipper AI feature (in-browser NPC dialogue via WebLLM, image-recognition games, voice-driven interfaces via Whisper.cpp WASM) that runs on client devices (sitepoint.com/webgpu-vs-webgl-inference-benchmarks Feb 25 2026); JavaScript Weekly 2026 Week 20 (cloud.tencent.com June 2026) — Node 26.1 experimental FFI support, Bun built-in headless-browser automation + cron scheduling, BlueJS AOT JS compiler (5ms startup, 3.8MB peak memory, 1.2MB binary), Astro 6.3 experimental advanced routing, Valibot 1.4.0 TypeScript schema-validation library — the 2026-MIDYEAR-JS-RUNTIME-AND-TOOLING-EVOLUTION dimension distinct from W-159 (Rolldown 1.0 + Vite 8 build-toolchain migration — bundler) and C-110 (browser DevTools console CVE-scanner) because G-148 covers RUNTIME-FEATURE and TOOLING changes affecting future GameZipper build-pipeline choices (cloud.tencent.com/developer/article/2679559 June 2026 JavaScript Weekly #20).
**Version Note**: v1.76.0 = R196 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 7 new test cases: CVE-2026-32041 OpenClaw browser-control startup UNAUTHENTICATED-after-auth-bootstrap-failure (March 2 2026 GHSA-vpj2-69hf-rppw GitHub Advisory CVSS HIGH) — the AI-AGENT-FAILURE-MODE-CRITICAL-PRIVILEGE-CONTINUATION dimension distinct from S-128 (OpenClaw ClawHub marketplace-malware — third-party skill) and S-129 (ClickFix macOS AppleScript delivery — social engineering) because S-146 is the FAIL-OPEN-on-bootstrap-error dimension — a local process or loopback-reachable SSRF path could access browser-control routes including evaluate-capable actions without authentication (github.com/advisories/GHSA-vpj2-69hf-rppw Mar 2 2026); CVE-2026-25253 OpenClaw ClawJacked URL-parameter WebSocket hijacking RCE (Jan 2026 disclosure, CVSS 8.8, 135,000+ public-exposed instances + 15,000+ at-RCE-risk per SecurityScorecard) — the AI-AGENT-URL-PARAMETER-WEBSOCKET-HIJACKING-AUTH-TOKEN-LEAK dimension distinct from S-146 (auth-bootstrap-fail-open) and S-141 (SANDWORM_MODE env-var-toggle) because S-147 chains THREE independent defects — unvalidated URL query param `gatewayUrl` persisted to localStorage + auto-trigger `connectGateway()` on lifecycle hook + WebSocket Origin-header validation gap — to produce a no-click RCE via single-malicious-link delivery (blog.csdn.net/sd7o95o/article/details/158105132 May 16 2026; blog.csdn.net/FreeBuf_/article/details/157685273 Feb 3 2026; blog.csdn.net/zhuimengkaifazhe/article/details/158975962 Mar 12 2026); OpenClaw CVE cluster CVE-2026-33573 + CVE-2026-41370 (Mar 29 2026 + Apr 28 2026 — both HIGH CVSS) — the AI-AGENT-VULNERABILITY-CLUSTER-ACCELERATION dimension distinct from prior OpenClaw entries (S-128 marketplace, S-146 bootstrap-fail, S-147 WS-hijack) because S-148 documents a SINGLE open-source AI-agent platform accumulating 4+ severe CVEs in <3 months with each having a DISTINCT ROOT CAUSE (workspace-boundary RPC bypass + path-traversal via ACP inbound-channel attachment + auth-bootstrap fail-open + URL-param WS hijack) — proving that AI-agent platforms at the OPERATING-SYSTEM-FOR-AGENTS layer (which Hermes/OpenClaw/PilotDeck W-179 are) carry a CVE-cluster risk that dwarfs traditional single-CVE-per-product browser/library CVEs; GameZipper QA pipeline that invokes Hermes/Kanban workers with broad filesystem+network tool access must treat the agent-platform CVE cluster as a recurring supply-chain risk (github.com/advisories/GHSA-5w3r-prr4-7j25 Mar 29 2026; github.com/advisories/GHSA-pmg8-9xxh-v2wv Apr 28 2026; vulncheck.com advisories); Mozilla + Anthropic Mythos Preview = 271 Firefox bugs in 2 months (180 high-severity + 80 medium + 11 low) — Mozilla DIRECTLY published a blog post May 8 2026 confirming the volume to address "AI 找 Bug 质疑" (AI-found-bug skepticism); the AI-DISCOVERY-VOLUME-VENDOR-DIRECTLY-CORROBORATED-FIREFOX-SIDE dimension distinct from S-113 (Mythos 100 Chromium zero-days in 15 days — Chromium-side, defensive-disclosure) because S-149 is the FIREFOX-SIDE vendor-confirmed corroboration that AI-discovery volume is REAL not hallucinated (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_53769fd53ba77252 May 8 2026 Mozilla blog + 财联社 reporting); Anthropic Claude Fable 5 + Mythos 5 first PUBLIC Mythos-class model release (June 9 2026, SWE-bench Pro 80.3%, 1M token context, $10/$50 per Mtok input/output pricing) — Fable 5 is Mythos 5's safety-aligned version, available to ALL users including the public; the AI-VULN-GENERATION-CAPABILITY-MADE-PUBLICLY-AVAILABLE dimension distinct from C-114 (Anthropic Mythos vuln-GENERATION capability announcement — INTERNAL-only) because W-180 is the GENERAL-AVAILABILITY release of a Mythos-class model — anyone can now invoke a Mythos-tier model to generate phishing UIs (C-116), exploit chains (C-114 offensive counterpart), or malware obfuscation that defeats existing GameZipper supply-chain scanners; GameZipper must extend its anti-phishing and anti-supply-chain scanners to assume attackers have Mythos-class tooling (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_9726a28dde799552 June 10 2026; blog.csdn.net/msbcsdn/article/details/161860642 June 10 2026; blog.csdn.net/nmdbbzcl/article/details/161870199 June 16 2026); Apple MIE (Memory Integrity Enforcement) deployed on M5 Mac + A19 iPhone (Feb 4 2026 Apple Platform Security Guide update) — the HARDWARE-LEVEL-MEMORY-SAFETY-LAYER-FOR-BROWSER-GAMES dimension distinct from B-097 (iOS 26.5 advisory that MENTIONS M5 bypass as one of 80+ vulns) because B-113 documents MIE itself as the new HARDWARE GUARANTEE LAYER — MIE constrains attacker ability to exploit memory-corruption vulnerabilities, providing persistent memory safety for kernel + critical attack surfaces; affects GameZipper WASM-compiled games on M5 Macs + iPhone A19 devices where MIE silently protects against class-of-bugs that prior chip generations were vulnerable to (new.qq.com/rain/a/20260204A063IK00 Feb 4 2026); Calif researchers used Claude Mythos Preview to break Apple M5 macOS kernel MIE in 5 days (May 17-21 2026, 5-year $billions defense bypassed, 2 chained unpatched bugs, root shell from unprivileged local user) — the AI-ASSISTED-HARDWARE-MEMORY-SAFETY-BYPASS-CONCRETE-PROOF-OF-CONCEPT dimension distinct from C-114 (Mythos capability announcement) and B-097 (M5 bypass mentioned in advisory) because P-072 is the CONCRETE PROOF that AI assistance defeats HARDWARE-LEVEL memory-safety guarantees that took 5 years + billions to build — meaning the MIE hardware guarantees documented in B-113 are NOT a permanent defense, just a speed bump that Mythos-class AI can defeat in days not years; GameZipper games that assume M5/A19 hardware memory safety will fail-open when Mythos-tier exploit-generation becomes routine (blog.csdn.net/qq_73472828/article/details/161193885 May 21 2026; jdon.com/92074-first-public-kernel-exploit-apple-m5-mythos-previe.html May 17 2026).
**Version Note**: v1.75.0 = R195 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 6 new test cases: CVE-2026-41008 Spring Security Authorization Server Open Redirect via request_uri (June 9 2026 spring.io MEDIUM CVSS) — the OAUTH-PAR-OPEN-REDIRECT dimension distinct from prior OAuth coverage because CVE-2026-41008 is the PUSHED-AUTHORIZATION-REQUEST (PAR) request_uri parameter insufficient validation that allows attacker-crafted invalid request_uri + arbitrary unvalidated redirect_uri to chain into an Open Redirect — affects any future GameZipper OAuth/login flow that delegates to Spring Security Authorization Server (spring.io/security/cve-2026-41008 June 9 2026); CVE-2026-2472 Google Cloud Vertex AI SDK stored XSS in `_genai/_evals_visualization.py` (Feb 20 2026 GCP-2026-011) — the AI-SDK-STORED-XSS-IN-EVALS-VISUALIZATION dimension distinct from C-113 vibe-coding (developer-side risk) because CVE-2026-2472 is the RUNTIME-side risk — an AI SDK that renders eval results in a browser-trusted page can be XSS-pwned if any eval payload contains attacker-controlled markup — affects any future GameZipper AI feature that visualizes LLM evals (github.com/JoshuaProvoste/CVE-2026-2472-Vertex-AI-SDK-Google-Cloud Feb 20 2026); iOS 26 Liquid Glass Control Center over-transparency regression (June 8 2026 tenorshare.tw + cnblogs user-adaptation logs) — the IOS-26-LIQUID-GLASS-OS-LEVEL-VISIBILITY-REGRESSION dimension distinct from existing iOS entries because B-112 is the OS-OVERLAY-VISIBILITY-CLASH dimension — iOS 26's default-on Liquid Glass makes Control Center over-transparent so any GameZipper game paused behind the Control Center has its canvas visually clash with the OS overlay → readability regression (tenorshare.tw/ios-update-problems June 8 2026; cnblogs.com/jiushi8888/p/19246072 Nov 20 2025 iOS-26 adaptation log); iOS 27 Liquid Glass transparency slider + auto-adapt to "Reduce Transparency"/"Increase Contrast" a11y settings (WWDC26 June 11 2026) — the IOS-27-LIQUID-GLASS-TRANSPARENCY-SLIDER-A11Y-AUTO-ADAPT dimension distinct from B-112 (iOS 26 over-transparency regression — the PROBLEM) because A-041 is the SOLUTION Apple shipped in iOS 27 — continuous transparency slider ("超清晰" to "完全着色") + auto-adapt to OS a11y preferences (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_4876a2a514b51852 June 11 2026); Safari 27 beta ship with 58 new features + customizable `<select>` + scroll anchoring + WebAssembly JSPI (June 9 2026 webkit.org "News from WWDC26: WebKit in Safari 27 beta" + web-standards.dev June 9 2026) — the SAFARI-27-BETA-STABLE-MILESTONE-WEBKIT-FEATURES-COUNT dimension distinct from W-129 (Safari 27 AI tab organization — single feature) and B-090 (Safari 27 WWDC26 525 fixes — fix-count only) because C-118 covers the 58-NEW-FEATURES surface that needs QA — customizable `<select>` (CSS-native stylable select), scroll anchoring (GameZipper footer scrolling), WebAssembly JSPI (async WASM call ABI for compute-heavy AI/game loops) (webkit.org/blog June 9 2026; web-standards.dev June 9 2026); Tsinghua PilotDeck open-source Agent OS May 28 2026 (OpenBMB/THUNLP/ModelBest/AI9Stars AGPL-3.0 TypeScript) — the MEMORY-EDITABLE-AGENT-OS-WITH-PER-TASK-TOKEN-TRACKING-AND-MODEL-ROUTER dimension distinct from existing OpenClaw/Mythos/SANDWORM dimensions because W-179 is the BENEIGN-CHINESE-ACADEMIC open-source agent OS dimension — memory-whitebox-editable + per-task token tracking + automatic model routing by difficulty; relevant to GameZipper QA pipeline economics because PilotDeck-class agent OSes may run GameZipper puzzle-pipeline tasks and the per-task token tracking + memory-editability dimensions affect QA pipeline cost accounting + memory-debugging patterns (github.com/OpenBMB/PilotDeck May 28 2026; cnblogs.com/itech/p/20199005 May 28 2026).
**Version Note**: v1.74.0 = R194 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 6 new test cases: Chrome 148 Android approximate-location permission toggle (May 7 2026 IT之家) — Chrome 148 Android shipped a new privacy setting that lets a website use APPROXIMATE location rather than PRECISE location; the APPROXIMATE-vs-PRECISE-LOCATION-PERMISSION-TOGGLE dimension distinct from S-103 (Privacy Sandbox killed — cross-site ad/analytics tracking) because W-176 is the GRANULARITY-of-consented-geolocation dimension not the cross-site-tracking-cookies dimension; affects any future GameZipper game that uses navigator.geolocation (geo-puzzle, weather-themed game) — QA must verify graceful behavior when user grants only approximate location, not precise (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_83069fc56b987652 May 7 2026); Apple App Store AI-Agent Marketplace integration (WWDC26 June 9 2026 keynote + The Information pre-leak June 5 2026) — Apple plans to integrate AI agents (智能体) into the App Store as a first-party distribution surface, allowing users to delegate booking, document-editing, smart-home-control and other tasks to AI agents downloaded from the App Store; the APPLE-FIRST-PARTY-AI-AGENT-MARKETPLACE dimension distinct from S-128 (OpenClaw ClawHub AI-agent-marketplace supply-chain — a MALICIOUS third-party marketplace) because W-177 is Apple's BENIGN-FIRST-PARTY AI-agent store that nonetheless expands the AI-agent-as-distribution-channel surface any future GameZipper iOS app or PWA wrapper must compete with (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_8716a2269eb24452 June 5 2026; so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_2706a2207c793752 June 5 2026); Gemini 3.5 Flash generates interactive web UI (Google I/O May 20 2026) — Google I/O 2026 announced Gemini 3.5 Flash can generate \"更丰富、更具交互性的网页用户界面与图形\" (richer, more interactive web UIs and graphics); the AI-GENERATED-INTERACTIVE-WEB-UI-FROM-PROMPT dimension distinct from C-095 (HTML-in-Canvas API — a RENDERING API) and C-113 (vibe coding — human-curated AI code) because C-116 is a USER-FACING-LLM-GENERATED-INTERACTIVE-UI dimension where a malicious prompt could produce a phishing UI indistinguishable from a legitimate GameZipper page (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_9086a0d898b68852 May 20 2026; blog.csdn.net/2601_96073073/article/details/161245172 June 17 2026); Google Antigravity 2.0 + Android CLI + AI code-migration agent (Google I/O May 20 2026) — Google I/O 2026 shipped Antigravity 2.0 (agentic dev platform), Android CLI, and a code-migration agent that auto-migrates legacy codebases (jQuery→React, Vue 2→Vue 3, legacy ES5→ES2026); the AI-DRIVEN-CODE-MIGRATION dimension distinct from C-104 (Codex CLI as QA tool — security constraint when using Codex) because C-117 is the REVERSE-CVE risk — an AI migration agent refactoring previously-working legacy code introduces NEW bugs that deterministic QA may not catch; GameZipper's recurring 55-game rAF-cancelAnimationFrame template bug (v1.59.0) and BGM-setInterval leak bug are exactly the kind of latent bug an AI-migration pass would amplify (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_9086a0d898b68852 May 20 2026; blog.csdn.net/hanzhixintianxia/article/details/161265277 June 4 2026); Bank of America \"agentic-phone era\" analyst validation (June 11 2026 CaiLianShe) — BoA's post-WWDC26 analyst report explicitly framed iOS 27 as the start of the 智能体手机 (agentic-phone) era; the ANALYST-VALIDATED-AGENTIC-PHONE-INFLECTION-POINT dimension distinct from W-170 (2026 cybersecurity report — threat-landscape) and B-108 (iOS 27 Siri AI standalone app — feature) because W-178 is a MARKET-STRUCTURAL signal that affects QA budget allocation — if a Tier-1 bank validates the agentic-phone inflection, GameZipper's QA roadmap must allocate dedicated agentic-surface coverage (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_1436a2aa8ad65652 June 11 2026 CaiLianShe); iOS 27 Siri cross-app context search + iCloud conversation history (WWDC26 June 9 2026) — Siri AI can \"可以理解屏幕上的内容 可以结合个人上下文回答问题 可以跨 app 搜索信息、邮件、照片等内容\" (understand screen content, combine with personal context, search across apps for mail/photos/etc); the SIRI-CROSS-APP-CONTEXT-AWARE-ON-DEVICE-AI dimension distinct from B-108 (iOS 27 Siri AI standalone app — discovery surface) and P-071 (iOS 27 12GB RAM minimum for AI features — hardware gating) because B-111 is the PRIVACY surface — a player mid-GameZipper-game can ask Siri to mine context from their Mail/Photos/Messages about the game they're viewing, exposing GameZipper to indirect-AI-context-leak vectors; QA must verify no GameZipper page leaks sensitive player data via document structure that Siri's on-device summarization surfaces to a third party (blog.csdn.net/aifengaopeng/article/details/161833067 June 9 2026; new.qq.com/rain/a/20260609A025RF00 June 9 2026).
**Version Note**: v1.73.0 = R193 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 6 new test cases: AMD FFmpeg "AI slop" patches Jan 30 2026 — FFmpeg maintainers publicly rejected AMD's pull request for "AI-generated junk" (AI slop) commit messages; the AI-GENERATED-PATCH SUPPLY-CHAIN dimension distinct from S-124 (Glassworm invisible-Unicode source-text-level obfuscation — hides malicious logic in invisible Unicode chars) because S-142 hides a backdoor in AI-slop STYLING that exploits maintainer fatigue — an attacker submits an "AI-slop-looking" PR that maintainers rubber-stamp to clear their queue; distinct from S-141 (SANDWORM_MODE env-var-toggle runtime evasion) because S-142 is SOCIAL-ENGINEERING-of-maintainer-trust, not runtime-conditional logic; distinct from S-126 (eslint-config-prettier maintainer-phishing) because S-142 does NOT phish the maintainer — it submits code that LOOKS like benign AI-slop so the maintainer dismissively merges it (new.qq.com/rain/a/20260130A067F100 Jan 30 2026; so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_186697c803488452 Jan 30 2026); AI-FLOODED-BUG-TRIAGE-QUEUE — Google overhauled Chrome bounty program (Chrome 149 record 429-CVE batch June 6 2026 thehackernews.com) to cope with AI-generated-report flood + Google Project Zero Big Sleep publicly shamed FFmpeg maintainers Nov 9 2025 to force rushed patch timelines; the AI-DISCLOSURE-DEADLINE-PRESSURE dimension distinct from S-057 (20h exploit window SLA) because S-143 is the OPPOSITE direction — AI-discovered bugs are publicly exposed forcing rushed patches that may introduce regressions, distinct from S-113 (Anthropic Mythos defensive discovery) and C-114 (Mythos offensive generation) because S-143 is the TRIAGE-DENIAL-OF-SERVICE dimension where AI-generated reports flood manual queues (thehackernews.com/search/label/Chrome June 6 2026; so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_5096910234518252 Nov 9 2025); Meta 4× AI bug detection methodology April 22 2026 — Meta published a methodology for AI-assisted bug detection that improved bug detection rate by 4×, counter to the AI-generated-code-bypass (C-113) — the AI-ASSISTED-QA-METHODOLOGY dimension distinct from C-100/C-103 (self-healing locator stability — false-pass detection) because C-115 is a COVERAGE-INCREASE methodology not a locator-stability issue, distinct from C-098 (WebGPU "break on error" DevTools debug protocol) because C-115 is a Meta-validated real-codebase result not a spec recommendation (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_40169e85c7b39652 April 22 2026); METR RCT April 25 2026 — independent research institution METR published a randomized controlled trial finding experienced open-source developers using AI coding tools were 19% SLOWER not faster, but SUBJECTIVELY PERCEIVED they were faster; the AI-CODING-PRODUCTIVITY-ILLUSION dimension distinct from C-113 (vibe-coding AI-generated-code nondeterminism — QUALITY risk) because W-174 is the QUANTITY/PRODUCTIVITY decision-basis dimension — GameZipper pipeline estimates based on "AI makes us 2× faster" are biased in the OPPOSITE direction (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_56169ec6f4976052 April 25 2026); iOS 27 Safari "Notify Me" + "Create Extension" June 12 2026 — iOS 27 Safari adds user-AI-generated extensions (natural-language prompt → AI generates a custom extension) AND a "Notify Me" continuous webpage-monitoring feature that polls pages for content changes; the iOS-27-SAFARI-USER-AI-EXTENSION-AND-NOTIFY-ME-NEW-POLLING-SURFACE dimension distinct from W-129 (Safari 27 Apple Intelligence tab organization — tab reordering) because W-175 covers AI-GENERATED-ON-DEVICE EXTENSIONS that modify page behavior (not tab reordering) AND a new periodic-polling load surface, distinct from S-128 (OpenClaw ClawHub AI-agent-marketplace supply-chain) and W-173 (Chrome MV3 typosquatting window) because W-175 extensions are USER-GENERATED ON-DEVICE BY APPLE-AI — possibly unreviewed by Apple, generated from a natural-language prompt that could itself be prompt-injected; Notify Me polling means N iOS-Safari users monitoring a GameZipper page generate N periodic pollers (new.qq.com/rain/a/20260613A02J4P00 June 13 2026 MacRumors June 12 2026); iOS 27 12GB RAM minimum for advanced local AI features WWDC26 June 9 2026 — DoNews reports some iOS 27 advanced Apple Intelligence features require 12GB+ RAM devices (iPhone Air etc.); the iOS-RAM-TIER-DIVIDE-FOR-AI-FEATURES dimension distinct from P-048 (entry-tier Mali-G57/Adreno Android GPU baseline — graphics perf) because P-071 is the iOS-RAM dimension specifically for AI workloads, distinct from B-107 (Android 17 memory-pressure enforcement — Android platform behavior) because P-071 is Apple-platform AI-feature gating; GameZipper future AI features (NPC/voice/image via WebLLM/WebGPU LLM inference) will silently fail/degrade on <12GB iPhones (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_5106a275b0d01252 June 9 2026 DoNews).
**Version Note**: v1.72.0 = R192 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 6 new test cases: CVE-2026-20700 iOS dyld (Dynamic Link editor) zero-day patched in iOS 26.3 Feb 12 2026 — affects every iOS version since 1.0 (decade-old 19-year bug), Google TAG discovered, "extremely sophisticated attack against specific targeted individuals", patched in iOS 26.3 — the iOS-DYLD-LINKER-DECADE-OLD-EXPLOIT-CHAIN dimension distinct from B-110 (China NVDB iOS-WebKit red alert — WebKit rendering layer) because CVE-2026-20700 is the OS-level dynamic-linker layer BELOW WebKit, requires memory-write precondition so it is an EXPLOIT-CHAIN final stage not a no-click zero-day; distinct from B-097 (iOS 26.5 generic patch-list) which is generic (theregister.com Feb 12 2026; blog.csdn.net/smellycat000/article/details/158033092 Feb 12 2026); CVE-2026-3910 + CVE-2026-3909 Chrome V8 + Skia dual-0day pair (March 18 2026) — Google紧急修复Chrome浏览器中两个遭野外利用的0Day漏洞: CVE-2026-3910 (V8 JavaScript/WebAssembly engine sandbox-bypass → arbitrary code execution via crafted HTML) + CVE-2026-3909 (Skia graphics library OOB write via crafted HTML) — both ACTIVELY EXPLOITED IN THE WILD; the DUAL-0DAY-V8-AND-SKIA-PAIR-ACTIVELY-EXPLOITED dimension distinct from S-115 (Chrome 150 DevTools blank-canvas visual-regression-test — different subsystem) and S-088 (CVE-2026-5281 Dawn WebGPU UAF) because S-140 is a PAIRED V8 + Skia exploit chain — a malicious Monetag/AdSense creative could chain both to escape the sandbox then corrupt rendering (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_87569ba00d309252 March 18 2026); SANDWORM_MODE npm supply-chain worm (Feb 28 2026, QQ News / Socket research) — 19+ impersonation packages with environment-variable-based runtime control logic ("SANDWORM_*" env vars toggle malicious behavior in shipped code so the package looks benign during automated review (env vars not set in scanner sandbox) but activates in production (env var present in CI/user environment); multi-stage payload stealing local environment + CI secrets → uses stolen tokens to MODIFY OTHER REPOS (propagation via stolen write access); ENV-VARIABLE-TOGGLED-RUNTIME-EVASION dimension distinct from S-131 (Axios version-history-forgery) and S-124 (Glassworm invisible-Unicode source-text-level obfuscation) because SANDWORM_MODE uses runtime env-var-conditional logic so static code review sees the env-var check and assumes it's a feature flag, not realizing the env-var-present path IS the malicious payload (new.qq.com/rain/a/20260228A047F100 Feb 28 2026); Anthropic Mythos AI vuln-GENERATION model (April 8 2026 Anthropic statement, corroborated June 9 2026 Claude Fable 5 leak) — Anthropic confirmed it developed "Mythos", an AI model that can GENERATE zero-day exploits, decided NOT to release because it would "破坏互联网" (break the internet); AI-VULN-GENERATION-CAPABILITY-MATURITY dimension distinct from S-113 (Anthropic Mythos 100 Chromium zero-days in 15 days — defensive DISCOVERY) because C-114 confirms the OFFENSIVE counterpart — raises QA cadence stakes for any web-game that ships a feature Mythos-class AI could find exploitable (so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_45769d629dd14252 April 8 2026; so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_2156a27d43031152 June 9 2026); Android XR + Project Aura first consumer WebXR AR glasses shipping 2026 (Google I/O May 19 2026 + XREAL May 20 2026 + W3C WebXR AR Module Level 1 + Meta IWSDK May 12 2026) — first Android XR AR glasses opens a new immersive browser-game surface; ANDROID-XR-WEBXR-AR-FORM-FACTOR dimension distinct from C-073 (foldable display viewport resize) and P-029 (120Hz VRR rendering) which are non-XR mobile, and from existing WebGPU dimensions because WebXR is a different rendering pipeline (immersive sessions, motion controllers, eye tracking); Chrome MV3 migration typosquatting window (163.com Jan 2025 EditThisCookie???? + new.qq.com June 16 2026 final kill switch) — when legitimate MV2 extensions (EditThisCookie, uBlock Origin) are delisted from Chrome Web Store during the forced MV3 migration, malicious MV3-compliant typosquatted clones (EditThisCookie????) appear and capture the abandoned user base (30K-50K users captured by single clone); MV3-MIGRATION-TYPOSQUATTING-WINDOW dimension distinct from W-151 (Manifest V2 final hard kill Chrome 150/151 June 30 2026 — the kill DATE) because W-173 is the SIDE-EFFECT of the kill — typosquatting attack-window created when legitimate extensions disappear.
**Version Note**: v1.71.0 = R191 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 6 new test cases: China NVDB (工信部 国家网络与信息安全信息通报中心 的下属平台 NVDB) iOS-WebKit RED ALERT (红色预警, April 3 2026) — foreign-APT in-the-wild exploitation of iOS WebKit, 42,000+ iPhones confirmed compromised in CN, single malicious webpage → no-interaction RCE → full device control — the SOVEREIGN-CERT-IOS-WEBKIT-RED-ALERT dimension distinct from W-167 (CN Shai-Hulud npm advisory — npm-specific) because B-110 is iOS-WebKit-specific and RED-ALERT (highest) level, and distinct from B-097 (iOS 26.5 generic 80+ vuln + 2 zero-days + M5 hardware advisory) because B-110 is a SPECIFIC national-red-alert response to foreign-APT exploitation confirmed across 42K+ devices; directly relevant to GameZipper's ~20% iOS Safari traffic — a malicious Monetag/AdSense creative could exploit whichever WebKit CVE is current (blog.csdn.net/xixixi7777/article/details/160202543 June 11 2026; new.qq.com/rain/a/20260119A01E5F00 Jan 19 2026; new.qq.com/rain/a/20251229A06B2H00 Dec 29 2025); CVE-2025-14174 / CVE-2025-43529 Cross-Vendor ANGLE Memory-OOB shared CVE (Apple Jan 19 2026; Google Threat Analysis Group discovered; both in-the-wild exploited) — CVE-2025-14174 is an ANGLE memory-OOB that affects BOTH iOS Safari/WebKit (via ANGLE Metal backend) AND Chrome/Chromium (via ANGLE DirectX/Vulkan backend) — the CROSS-VENDOR-ANGLE-SHARED-CVE dimension: one graphics-library-layer CVE propagates to multiple browser vendors simultaneously, so a patched-Chrome-but-unpatched-iOS (or vice versa) means the SAME exploit creative can hit whichever platform lags (cnblogs.com/clnchanpin/p/19475745 Jan 13 2026; support.apple.com/en-us/125884); Safari 26 stable ship + last-minute privacy-feature PULLBACK (cloud.tencent.com June 19 2026) — WebKit bug 134226694 (auto-strip gclid/fbclid tracking params in links in regular browsing mode) + WebKit bug 134227067 (prevent fingerprinting for known tracking scripts) were BOTH announced for Safari 26 beta but REMOVED from the final Safari 26 stable ship — the SAFARI-ANNOUNCED-PRIVACY-FEATURE-PULLED-LAST-MINUTE dimension: any privacy feature Apple ANNOUNCES in Safari beta is at-risk-of-pullback; QA analytics plans cannot rely on beta-announced features until stable ship (cloud.tencent.com/developer/article/2637644 June 19 2026); Safari 26.5 WebRTC process-crash fix (Apple May 14 2026) — the SAFARI-26.5-WEBRTC-CRASH-FIX dimension distinct from S-085 (CVE-2026-9111 WebRTC UAF RCE — Linux Chromium) because S-138 is Safari-specific WebRTC crash fix from a different bug class; any GameZipper game using WebRTC (peer-to-peer multiplayer, voice chat) on Safari 26/26.4 could crash mid-session (developer.apple.com/news May 14 2026 Safari 26.5 security list; so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_0236a054d4370952 May 14 2026); PHY041/phy-a11y-reviewer automated WCAG 2.2 AA web-application review tool (GitHub March 6 2026; corroborated by a11ycheck mrgig.com May 16 2026) — the AUTOMATED-WCAG-2.2-AA-REVIEWER-TOOLCHAIN dimension distinct from A-034 (US ADA Title II — legal/regulatory) and A-037 (iOS 27 Accessibility framework rewrite — Apple-API-specific) because A-040 is the TOOLING-AUTOMATION dimension — actionable integration of a specific open-source reviewer into the test pipeline (github.com/PHY041/phy-a11y-reviewer; mrgig.com); WebGPU 39x draw-call throughput + 2.75x texture-sampling rate vs WebGL2 (CSDN AWS WebGPU实战 Three.js March 24 2026; corroborated by zhihu WebGPU perf analysis Aug 2022) — CONCRETE-WebGPU-PERF-NUMBERS-FOR-MIGRATION-CASE dimension distinct from P-030 (WebGPU 70% coverage + 15x compute — coarse 15x) and P-047 (WebGPU 8.5x draw calls + 4.5x memory bandwidth — different metric/source) because P-069 provides the SPECIFIC draw-call-frame throughput number (470K vs 12K) + sampling rate (22 vs 8 GB/s) that sets a CONCRETE migration-decision threshold for any high-sprite-count game (bullet-hell, particle-heavy, large-tile puzzle) currently capping at WebGL2 limits (blog.csdn.net/awscloud/article/details/146470071 March 24 2026; zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/387054998 Aug 2 2022).

**Version Note** (legacy v1.68.0): v1.68.0 = R188 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 8 new test cases: React2Shell CVE-2025-55182 React Server Components Flight-Protocol CVSS-10.0 unauthenticated RCE (Dec 3 2025 disclosure, CISA KEV Dec 5, actively exploited Jan 2026 against insurance/e-commerce/IT + May 2026 mass-exploitation of Dify/Next.js-App-Router self-hosted AI platforms for mining + AI API-key theft + persistence backdoor; AI-PLATFORM-STACK-RSC-RCE dimension distinct from S-132 Langflow because the exploit surface is the React Flight wire protocol itself not an exec()-on-input design flaw, and distinct from S-130/S-135 npm attacks because this is a CVE in shipped library code not a malicious package) (S-133), CVE-2025-11953 React Native Metro dev-server local-network unauthenticated RCE CVSS 9.8 (JFrog Nov 5 2025; @react-native-community/cli-server-api 4.8.0-20.0.0-alpha.2; attacker on same network sends malicious HTTP to Metro → arbitrary OS commands on dev machine; DEV-ENVIRONMENT LOCAL-NETWORK RCE dimension distinct from S-133 production-server RCE) (S-134), CanisterWorm / Namastex Labs AI-agent-company-targeted npm worm with ICP decentralized-blockchain C2 exfil endpoint (Socket/StepSecurity April 24 2026; LEGACY-DAEMON PROTOCOL-PARSER OVERFLOW dimension) (W-162), CVE-2026-21992 Oracle Identity Manager / Web Services Manager unauthenticated HTTP RCE CVSS 9.8 (March 23 2026; IDENTITY-INFRASTRUCTURE UNAUTH-RCE dimension) (W-163), ChatGPT 2026 sponsored-content / AI-advertising (The Information Dec 25 2025 — OpenAI employees prototyping sponsored-result injection into ChatGPT answers; AI-CHATBOT OFFICIAL-SPONSORED-CONTENT DISCOVERY-DISPLACEMENT dimension distinct from S-120 ChatGPhish/S-127 cryptojacking because the displacement is BY THE VENDOR not an attacker) (W-164), WebGPU+WASM shared-memory architecture 2026 (tool.lu June 3 2026; WASM-linear-memory ↔ WebGPU-buffer shared-memory boundary is a NEW failure surface for browser games running physics in WASM + rendering in WebGPU) (P-067), browser-DevTools-console CVE-scanner tool pattern (cloud.tencent.com June 18 2026 GitHub CVE scanner for React/Next.js that runs by pasting untrusted JS into the github.com DevTools console; DEVTOOLS-CONSOLE-RUNNING-UNTRUSTED-JS dimension — a security tool itself becomes a vector when QA/devs paste untrusted JS into a console on a sensitive authenticated origin) (C-110).
**Version Note** (legacy v1.67.0): v1.67.0 = R187 Dynamic Test Intelligence cron — 8 new test cases: TanStack/Mistral AI "Mini Shai-Hulud" TeamPCP cross-registry npm+PyPI supply-chain attack (May 11 2026, 62 packages/404 malicious versions; @tanstack/react-router alone 12M+ weekly downloads; AI-AGENT-CONFIG-FILE-PERSISTENCE dimension distinct from every prior S-104~S-129 npm case because persistence lives in Claude Code `.claude/settings.json`, VS Code `.vscode/tasks.json`, Kiro/MCP OAuth tokens — NOT in node_modules or system daemons, so uninstalling the package does NOT clean it; DEAD-MAN-S-WITCH-WIPE-DAEMON dimension: if tokens revoked before host isolation, daemon wipes entire home directory as destructive anti-forensics; GitHub Actions OIDC trust-chain bypass with cryptographically valid SLSA Level 3 signatures; first TeamPCP campaign to harvest password managers 1Password/Bitwarden alongside cloud creds; reads /proc/pid/mem on Linux runners to extract even masked secrets; CSDN/Shai-Hulud-detect GitHub 3,290+ confirmed bad versions) (S-130), Axios npm supply-chain attack via VERSION-HISTORY-FORGERY (March 31 2026; attacker published clean `plain-crypto-js@4.2.0` first to forge a benign version-history, then dropped malicious `plain-crypto-js@4.2.1` 39 min before backdoored `axios@1.14.1` + `axios@0.30.4` so the malicious axios version looked like a routine bump; 300M+ weekly downloads; cross-platform RAT — macOS Mach-O `wt.exe`, Linux Python `/tmp/ld.py`; C2 sfrclak.com:8000 / IP 142.11.206.73; VERSION-HISTORY-FORGERY dimension distinct from S-126 maintainer-phishing because no human was phished — the attacker forged a clean version-history trail that automated review tools trust) (S-131), Langflow CVE-2026-33017 CVSS 9.3 AI-low-code-workflow-platform exec() RCE (Feb 26 2026 disclosed, exploited in 20h; POST /api/v1/build_public_tmp/{flow_id}/flow runs Python `exec()` on attacker-controlled `data` parameter with NO auth and NO sandbox; CISA KEV added, fix-by April 8 2026; AI-WORKFLOW-PLATFORM-EXEC-RCE dimension distinct from S-119 Marimo LLM-agent post-exploitation because Langflow is the PLATFORM-LEVEL exec() not post-compromise agent tool use; distinct from CVE-2025-3248 CVSS 9.8 which targeted /api/v1/validate/code — same root cause reused via a different endpoint) (S-132), Apifox desktop-client CDN poisoning 18-day credential harvest (April 2026 weekly security report; DESKTOP-DEV-TOOL-CDN-POISONING dimension distinct from every npm-registry case because the attack is against the tool's CDN update channel, not a package registry — every download during the 18-day window was compromised regardless of which version the user selected) (C-109), TA446 Russian APT first in-the-wild iOS/iCloud exploit-kit deployment DarkSword (April 2026 weekly report; iOS-APT-IN-THE-WILD dimension distinct from B-087 iOS 26 WebKit regression and B-097 iOS 26.5 advisory because those are PLATFORM BUGS whereas DarkSword is a deployed offensive kit targeting the mobile-Safari WebKit surface that casual-game players use) (B-105), Rolldown 1.0 + Vite 8 build-toolchain migration (RC Jan 21 2026, Vite 8 Beta Dec 4 2025, stable June 2026 per JS Weekly Week 20; Rust Rolldown replaces Rollup+esbuild; 10-30x faster than Rollup; `rollupOptions` → `rolldownOptions` and `esbuild` option → `oxc` option; BUILD-TOOLCHAIN-MIGRATION dimension distinct from B-103 Basemark URL migration because this is a config-API SHAPE CHANGE not a URL change; any GameZipper ad-stuff/3rd-party Vite-built artifact's bundle shape changes silently) (W-159), ShinyHunters EU Commission AWS breach 350GB exfiltrated (April 2026 weekly report; GOVERNMENT-CLOUD-BREACH-PUBLIC-SECTOR-RISK dimension distinct from A-034/A-035 ADA Title II deadlines because those are COMPLIANCE deadlines whereas ShinyHunters demonstrates ACTIVE-CLOUD-COMPROMISE of public-sector infrastructure — any GameZipper customer in the .gov/.edu space can have their Cloudflare/CDN credentials exfiltrated via a breached upstream provider) (W-160), Suno/Udio AI-music copyright lawsuit (June 24 2024 filing by UMG+Warner+Sony, ongoing in 2026; AI-GENERATED-AUDIO COPYRIGHT LITIGATION dimension distinct from W-146 FTC Honda dark-pattern because this is INTELLECTUAL-PROPERTY not consumer-protection; GameZipper games that use AI-generated BGM (Suno/MusicGen/AudioCraft per mlops skills) carry secondary-infringement risk if the AI model was trained on copyrighted recordings — a single DMCA takedown of the underlying model can render every shipped track retroactively infringing) (W-161).

June 11 2026 — PLATFORM-PROCUREMENT dimension; distinct from C-096/C-100/C-103 self-healing because this is vendor selection, not feature use) (C-105), IndonesianFoods npm spam-flood reputational-pollution technique (Nov 2025, Kaspersky Mar 2026 deep-dive — NOISE/REPUTATIONAL dimension attacking npm search + scanner signal-to-noise; distinct from S-104 credential-theft escalation which is the same campaign's other half) (W-156).

distinct from S-097/S-101 AI-as-target because this is AI-as-attacker-tool dimension; thehackernews.com June 2026) (S-119), Self-healing test-automation SILENT-false-pass detection — self-healing locators matching a renamed-but-similar button mask real regressions (action drift); distinct from C-096/C-097/C-100 self-healing introduction because this is the detection-mitigation angle (semantic assertions on top of locator matches) (qatechtools.com/vericence.com/scrolltest.com 2026) (C-103), Codex-CLI-as-QA-tool security constraint — when using Codex CLI as ACP transport for game QA (kanban-codex-lane pattern) the codexui-android compromise means Codex auth tokens in CI must be vault-rotated + least-privileged; tooling-up QA must not introduce a new attack surface (operational constraint dimension of S-116) (C-104), iOS 27 / iPadOS 27 / macOS Golden Gate WWDC26 stable-ship full-stack regression (June 2026 — Safari 27 stable ships, App Intents migration required for Siri 2.0, Universal Links v3, App Clips changes; distinct from B-090/S-096 WWDC26 beta because this is the POST-WWDC STABLE-SHIP milestone) (W-155), iOS 27 Accessibility framework rewrite — VoiceOver notification-priority changes, new Switch Control, rewritten Accessibility API; game canvas live regions must use updated APIs; distinct from A-034/A-035 general WCAG because this is iOS-27-STABLE-A11Y-FRAMEWORK-CHANGE dimension (apple.com WWDC26 + developer.apple.com accessibility sessions June 2026) (A-037).

distinct from G-124 WASM Component Model these are the LANGUAGE-FEATURE flags (A-033 → W-150, alldevtoolshub.com/dev.to) (W-150), UX as the Boss Fight 2026 (ContextSDK) — UX determines product viability not feature set; ## Severity Definitions (per user policy 2026-06-06: 100% fix rate P0-P3)
- **P0 (Blocker)**: Website inaccessible, game fails to load, game crashes, unplayable
- **P1 (Critical)**: Core gameplay broken, level uncompletable, data errors, monetization broken
- **P2 (Major)**: Secondary function anomalies, UI display issues, poor experience
- **P3 (Minor)**: Text errors, choppy animations, unclear prompts, minor cosmetic

---

## CATEGORY 1: WEBSITE-LEVEL TESTING (apply to all pages)

### 1.1 Homepage (/)
- [W-001] **[P0]** Homepage loads HTTP 200 in <2s
- [W-002] **[P0]** All game cards render (no missing thumbnails, no broken images)
- [W-003] **[P0]** Click on game card navigates to correct /game-slug/ URL
- [W-004] **[P0]** Logo click returns to homepage
- [W-005] **[P0]** Category filter buttons (All, Puzzle, Arcade, Idle, Card, Match-3) work and filter correctly
- [W-006] **[P1]** NEW badge displays correctly on games marked isNew:true
- [W-007] **[P1]** No JS console errors on homepage (zero-tolerance policy)
- [W-008] **[P1]** gz-error-toast "Something went wrong" does NOT show on normal load
- [W-009] **[P1]** Games count matches /js/games-data.js GAMES array length
- [W-010] **[P2]** Browser forward/back buttons work correctly
- [W-011] **[P2]** 404 page renders for non-existent URLs
- [W-012] **[P2]** Mobile responsive (375×667, 414×896 portrait/landscape)
- [W-013] **[P3]** Infinite scroll loads more games smoothly
- [W-014] **[P3]** Search box filters games by name

### 1.2 Network Performance
- [W-020] **[P0]** Homepage total payload ≤ 2MB
- [W-021] **[P0]** No zombie/dead external script src (1ktower.com, alwingulla.com, rye.io, monetag.com direct)
- [W-022] **[P0]** No mixed-active-content (HTTPS page must not reference http://)
- [W-023] **[P1]** All external script srcs return 200 (not 405, 404, 501)
- [W-024] **[P1]** DNS resolution time < 500ms
- [W-025] **[P2]** Cloudflare CDN cache hit rate > 80%
- [W-026] **[P2]** gz-analytics.js localStorage archive works (gz_aa, gz_ab)

### 1.3 SEO & Meta
- [W-030] **[P1]** Each page has unique <title> tag
- [W-031] **[P1]** Each page has meta description
- [W-032] **[P2]** JSON-LD structured data validates (VideoGame schema)
- [W-033] **[P2]** og:image present and not 404
- [W-034] **[P3]** Canonical link present
- [W-035] **[P1]** Chrome 130+ Document Picture-in-Picture (PiP) — when user activates PiP on game page, gameplay canvas continues rendering and accepting input; no "page hidden → rAF pause" failure
- [W-036] **[P1]** Chrome Performance Personalization (Memory Saver / Energy Saver) — game must remain playable when browser throttles background tabs. Resource hints (preconnect/preload) should not break under memory pressure. Source: Chrome 130 release notes (https://developer.chrome.com/release-notes/130)

## CATEGORY 2: INDIVIDUAL GAME TESTING (per game)

### 2.1 Game Loading & Setup
- [G-001] **[P0]** /game-slug/ returns HTTP 200 in <3s
- [G-002] **[P0]** Game has index.html with 3 essential elements:
  - `<div id="gz-ad-below-game">` (ad container)
  - `<script src="/monetag-manager.js">` (ad loader)
  - `<script src="/game-footer.js" defer>` (related games)
- [G-003] **[P0]** Game's main JS file (game.js or bundle) is correctly referenced in index.html
- [G-004] **[P0]** No JS error on first load (network error, 404, 405, SyntaxError)
- [G-005] **[P0]** No zombie endpoints (1ktower.com, alwingulla.com, rye.io)
- [G-006] **[P0]** No dead site-analytics.gamezipper.com 1x1 img
- [G-007] **[P1]** Splash screen (if present) dismisses within 8s or on click/touch/key
- [G-008] **[P1]** No site-analytics.gamezipper.com 1x1 pixel (HTTP 501)
- [G-009] **[P1]** All input event handlers registered (mousedown/touchstart/keydown/pointerdown)
- [G-010] **[P1]** init() function called after DOM ready
- [G-011] **[P1]** rAF cancelAnimationFrame correctly delegates to native
- [G-012] **[P1]** Game has visible H1 with game name
- [G-013] **[P2]** Canvas size scales to viewport (not stuck at 300x150)
- [G-014] **[P2]** Touch events work on mobile (mobile Safari, Android Chrome)
- [G-015] **[P2]** Keyboard events work (Arrow keys, Space, P, Esc)
- [G-016] **[P3]** No layout shift on load (CLS < 0.1)

### 2.2 Core Gameplay (per game type)

#### Puzzle (block/tile/grid)
- [GP-001] **[P0]** Grid elements in correct positions
- [GP-002] **[P0]** Match-3 / sort detection works
- [GP-003] **[P1]** Visual feedback on move (highlight, snap, animation)
- [GP-004] **[P1]** Hint/undo button works
- [GP-005] **[P1]** Level completion → next level transition
- [GP-006] **[P1]** Win/lose state detection
- [GP-007] **[P2]** Score calculation correct
- [GP-008] **[P2]** Score multiplier triggers correctly
- [GP-009] **[P3]** Game duration 3-5 min per session (casual standard)

#### Arcade (action/runner/shooter)
- [GA-001] **[P0]** Movement responds to key press / click
- [GA-002] **[P0]** Collision detection works
- [GA-003] **[P0]** Score updates in real-time
- [GA-004] **[P0]** Game-over / lives / restart mechanism
- [GA-005] **[P1]** Visual feedback smooth (≥60fps)
- [GA-006] **[P1]** Difficulty progression
- [GA-007] **[P2]** Combo system works
- [GA-008] **[P2]** No invincibility / one-hit-kill / infinite score bug

#### Card (solitaire/poker/blackjack)
- [GC-001] **[P0]** All cards render correctly (52 cards, or deck size)
- [GC-002] **[P0]** Card drag/click responds
- [GC-003] **[P1]** Score, moves, time tracked
- [GC-004] **[P1]** Win condition declares winner
- [GC-005] **[P1]** Undo/restart option
- [GC-006] **[P2]** Card animation smooth

#### Board (chess/checkers/backgammon)
- [GB-001] **[P0]** All pieces on board visible
- [GB-002] **[P0]** Turn mechanism (player vs AI)
- [GB-003] **[P1]** Move legality enforced
- [GB-004] **[P1]** Game-over and winner declaration
- [GB-005] **[P1]** New-game button
- [GB-006] **[P2]** AI difficulty levels

#### Idle (clicker)
- [GI-001] **[P0]** Auto-generation works without user input
- [GI-002] **[P0]** Click speeds up progression
- [GI-003] **[P1]** Upgrades purchasable
- [GI-004] **[P1]** Save state (localStorage) works across reload
- [GI-005] **[P1]** No offline earnings double-counting
- [GI-006] **[P2]** Prestige reset-with-bonus mechanic

#### Match-3

- [GM-002] **[P0]** Match detection (3+ same)
- [GM-003] **[P1]** Cascade after match
- [GM-004] **[P1]** Level progression

### 2.3 Multimedia System

- [GX-002] **[P0]** No autoplay issues (Chrome autoplay policy)
- [GX-003] **[P1]** Sound effects trigger correctly (click, score, win, lose)

- [GX-005] **[P1]** Volume control works

- [GX-007] **[P2]** Audio context resumed on first user gesture

- [GX-010] **[P2]** Vibration API haptic feedback — mobile games calling `navigator.vibrate(pattern)` on tap/win/lose should not throw; falls back silently on iOS Safari (no API support) and desktop. Source: 2026 mobile gaming UX trends (https://novatechbeacon.com/latest/why-2026-gaming-is-built-for-one-hand-play)

## CATEGORY 4: PERFORMANCE & STABILITY

### 4.1 Performance Metrics
- [P-001] **[P0]** Homepage load time ≤ 2s (LCP)

- [P-003] **[P0]** Memory usage during gameplay ≤ 500MB
- [P-004] **[P1]** CPU usage ≤ 30% during gameplay
- [P-005] **[P1]** FID (First Input Delay) ≤ 100ms
- [P-006] **[P1]** CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) ≤ 0.1
- [P-007] **[P2]** FCP (First Contentful Paint) ≤ 1.5s
- [P-008] **[P2]** TTI (Time to Interactive) ≤ 3s

### 4.2 Stability
- [P-010] **[P0]** 1-hour continuous gameplay: no crash, no freeze
- [P-011] **[P0]** No memory leak after 10 game switches
- [P-012] **[P1]** Normal recovery after browser minimize/maximize
- [P-013] **[P1]** Game state preserved after network interruption
- [P-014] **[P1]** Low-power mode (mobile): playable
- [P-015] **[P2]** Tab background 5min: resume without state loss

## CATEGORY 5: SECURITY & COMPLIANCE

### 5.1 Data Privacy
- [S-001] **[P0]** No collection of PII (email, phone, name)
- [S-002] **[P0]** No cookies used except for game progress
- [S-003] **[P0]** No third-party tracking scripts (only monetization-related)
- [S-004] **[P0]** No malicious code or pop-ups
- [S-005] **[P1]** localStorage usage is minimal and game-scoped

### 5.2 Content Compliance
- [S-010] **[P0]** All content suitable for all ages (no violence/porn/gambling)
- [S-011] **[P0]** No false advertising or misleading information
- [S-012] **[P0]** No intellectual property infringement
- [S-013] **[P1]** Privacy policy linked and accessible
- [S-014] **[P1]** Terms of service present
- [S-015] **[P2]** COPPA compliant (no data collection from children)

### 5.3 Security
- [S-020] **[P0]** No XSS vulnerabilities (all input sanitized)
- [S-021] **[P0]** No CSRF on form submissions
- [S-022] **[P1]** HTTPS enforced on all pages
- [S-023] **[P1]** No mixed-content (HTTP resources on HTTPS pages)
- [S-024] **[P2]** CSP header set appropriately
- [S-025] **[P2]** SRI for external scripts (if any)

### 6.1 Browser-Specific (Safari 26.4+)
- [B-001] **[P1]** IndexedDB works on Safari 26.4+ (was broken in iFrame)
- [B-002] **[P1]** GamePad API works with XBOX Cloud (fixed March 2026)
- [B-003] **[P2]** color-gamut P3 displays render correctly
- [B-004] **[P2]** prefers-color-scheme: dark mode handled
- [B-005] **[P0]** Chrome OOB GPU Write CVE-2026-9967 (pre-148.0.7778.216) — game.js using WebGL/WebGPU/Canvas2D must verify browser Chrome version ≥ 148.0.7778.216; failing build throws OOB GPU memory write. Source: https://stack.watch/browse/latest/
- [B-006] **[P0]** Chrome iOS Uninitialized Use CVE-2026-9963 (pre-148.0.7778.216) — Uninitialized Use allows RCE on game page. Any embedded iframe / WebGL context must run on patched Chrome. Source: https://stack.watch/browse/latest/
- [B-007] **[P1]** Safari 26.0 WebGPU enabled — games requesting `navigator.gpu` must not error on iOS 26+/iPadOS 26+ Safari 26.0; WebGL fallback path still required for pre-Safari 26 (Source: https://webkit.org/blog/17333/webkit-features-in-safari-26-0)
- [B-008] **[P2]** Safari 26.0 new CSS primitives (Anchor Positioning, Scroll-driven Animations, HDR images, SVG icons) — test rendering on iOS 26+/macOS 26+ Safari 26.0; fallbacks to static layout for older Safari

## CATEGORY 7: ACCESSIBILITY (WCAG 2.2 + GDC 2026 GASIG)

### 7.1 Visual Accessibility
- [A-001] **[P1]** prefers-color-scheme: dark mode — listens to system theme change in real time (not just initial load); OS-level dark/light toggle updates page colors without page reload. Source: https://accessibilitychecker.org/blog/dark-mode-accessibility
- [A-002] **[P2]** WCAG 2.2 color contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 for normal text, ≥ 3:1 for large text and UI components (buttons, score numbers, status text). Test on both light and dark backgrounds.

### 7.2 Motor & One-Hand UX (2026 mobile-first)
- [A-003] **[P1]** One-hand Thumb Zone: primary gameplay controls (move/shoot/jump/restart) and CTA buttons (Play Again, Next Level) are reachable within bottom 60% of viewport in portrait orientation on 375×667 / 414×896 phones. Source: https://novatechbeacon.com/latest/why-2026-gaming-is-built-for-one-hand-play

### 7.3 Cognitive & Screen Reader (GDC 2026 GASIG)
- [A-004] **[P2]** ARIA labels on canvas — game canvas has `role="application"` or `role="img"` + `aria-label="<Game Name>"` for screen readers (NVDA, VoiceOver, TalkBack). Source: https://igda-gasig.org/2026/04/01/gdc-2026-accessibility-roundtable

## CATEGORY 8: 2026-Q2 EMERGING THREATS (R89 — Dynamic Test Intelligence)

### 8.1 Browser Security CVEs (June 2026 disclosure wave)
- [B-009] **[P0]** Edge UXSS CVE-2026-45494 (CVSS 5.0, ZDI-26-330) — Microsoft Edge improper navigation event validation allows Universal XSS. Any game page with cross-origin iframes (e.g. embedded ad iframes, payment iframes) is exploitable on unpatched Edge. **Test**: confirm Edge ≥ 137.0.3296.93 with version check + verify X-Frame-Options / frame-ancestors CSP set to prevent UXSS amplification. Source: https://cyberpress.org/microsoft-edge-vulnerability
- [B-010] **[P0]** Edge Directory Traversal RCE CVE-2026-45495 (CVSS 7.5, ZDI-26-331) — Microsoft Edge cross-device managed sign-in path traversal enables RCE. **Test**: any game URL with Edge `ms-browser-extension://` or extension content must be on patched Edge. Source: https://www.zerodayinitiative.com/advisories/ZDI-26-331/
- [B-011] **[P0]** Chrome V8 Type Confusion CVE-2026-6363 (Chrome 147+ fix) — V8 JavaScript engine OOB memory write from type confusion, RCE on game page. **Test**: any game using JS-heavy animation (rAF, gameLoop, custom classes) must run on Chrome ≥ 147.0.7730.100 (or backport). Source: https://windowsforum.com/threads/cve-2026-6363-v8-type-confusion-chrome-147-fix-and-edge-patch-timeline.413791
- [B-012] **[P1]** Edge Origin Validation Bypass CVE-2026-45492 (CVSS 4.3, ZDI-26-329) — chainable with other vulns for RCE. **Test**: cross-origin POST endpoints on game pages must have CSRF token + SameSite=Strict cookies.

### 8.2 Safari 26.4 New Web Platform Features
- [B-013] **[P1]** Safari 26.4 WebTransport support (shipped) — low-latency UDP-based game server protocol available in iOS 26.4+/macOS 26.4+ Safari. **Test**: `if ('WebTransport' in window)` detection, fallback to WebSocket on older Safari. WebTransport unusable in iFrame per spec — must be top-level. Source: https://developmentstoday.com/news/safari-26-4-new-features-bug-fixes-2026
- [B-014] **[P2]** Safari 26.4 CSS Grid Lanes — magazine-style grid for game cards on homepage. **Test**: validate `display: grid; grid-template-columns: masonry;` fallback on pre-26.4 Safari. Source: https://developmentstoday.com/news/safari-26-4-new-features-bug-fixes-2026
- [B-015] **[P1]** Safari 26.4 Keyboard Lock API — fullscreen game can capture Escape/WASD without OS interruption. **Test**: request keyboard lock on game-start, gracefully release on blur/visibilitychange; desktop Safari only (no iOS).

### 8.3 Visual Bug Detection (AI-Assisted)
- [P-016] **[P1]** Canvas pixel-change assertion — automated visual regression test verifies canvas pixels change during game interaction (matches arXiv 2208.02335 methodology: 100% accuracy on 24 injected visual bugs vs 44.6% for traditional snapshot testing). **Test**: capture canvas pixels before + after 5s interaction, assert ≥ 0.5% pixel diff, then use vision_analyze to confirm semantic correctness. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2208.02335
- [P-017] **[P2]** Gameplay state diff (not just pixels) — text content + DOM state + score + level must all change during normal play. Pure pixel diff can false-positive on splash screens or pure background gradients.

### 8.4 Interop 2026 (Cross-Browser Convergence)
- [B-016] **[P2]** Interop 2026 baseline (Chrome/Firefox/Edge ≥ 98% baseline features, Safari 98%) — game must not depend on Safari/Edge divergence bugs. **Test**: identical gameplay verified across Chrome 149, Firefox 147, Edge 137, Safari 26.4. Source: https://howtogeek.com/chrome-firefox-edge-and-safari-are-teaming-up-to-fix-common-web-problems

### 8.5 Game Testing Methodology 2026 (SnoopGame / Sogeti / GDC)
- [PC-009] **[P2]** Shift-Right live playtesting — in-app feedback button on every game page (`?issue=1` query param) sends feedback to gz_aa localStorage archive. **Test**: feedback button visible on mobile, opens email/mailto with game slug pre-filled. Source: https://snoopgame.com/blog/top-game-testing-trends-to-watch-in-2026
- [PC-010] **[P2]** AI-driven QA smoke test — game should be playable in ≤ 60 seconds by an automated Playwright run (no tutorial wall, no forced login, no level select required).

## CATEGORY 9: PWA / OFFLINE / SERVICE WORKER

### 9.1 Service Worker Registration
- [PWA-001] **[P1]** No stale service worker blocks new game load — when a `sw.js` is updated, hard reload must show new content. **Test**: bump JS cache-bust version, hard reload, verify new version loads. GameZipper uses HTTP cache + meta refresh, not SW, so if SW is added in future, this is critical.
- [PWA-002] **[P2]** localStorage and IndexedDB persistence across page reload — high-score, level-progress, settings (mute, language) survive browser restart. **Test**: play game, set high-score, close tab, reopen, verify score persists. QuotaExceededError must be caught and UI must not crash.

## CATEGORY 10: 2026-Q2 LATE-EMERGING THREATS + NEW PLATFORM (R91 — Dynamic Test Intelligence)

### 10.1 Password Manager Clickjacking 2026 (DEF CON 33)
- [S-026] **[P0]** No clickjacking-style UI manipulation — game page must not use `opacity:0`, `pointer-events:none`, or invisible overlays on top of (or below) any element designed to trick users into clicking hidden targets. This includes: ad iframes, GAME BREAK overlay, splash-screen, cookie consent banner, recovery toast. **Test**: grep `gz-cb-overlay|gz-error-toast|splash-screen|cookie-consent` + audit computed style (must be `pointer-events:auto`, `visibility:visible`, `opacity>0` on clickable surfaces). Inspired by DEF CON 33 Marek Tóth disclosure showing 1Password, Bitwarden, LastPass, Enpass, iCloud Passwords, LogMeOnce all vulnerable to autofill data exfiltration. Source: https://dailysecurityreview.com/news/clickjacking-vulnerability-exposes-autofill-data-across-major-extensions
- [S-027] **[P0]** X-Frame-Options / CSP frame-ancestors enforced — no game page can be iframed by `attacker.com` for clickjacking. **Test**: `curl -sI https://gamezipper.com/GAME/` returns `X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN` OR CSP `frame-ancestors 'self'`. Without either, page is trivially iframe-wrapped for clickjacking amplification.

### 10.2 Chrome 149 + Password Manager CVE-2026-11084
- [B-017] **[P0]** Chrome 149+ required for production — Chrome 149 promoted to stable 2026-05 with 429 security patches (a record, including 22 critical + many use-after-free in untrusted input handling) + CVE-2026-11084 (Password Manager cross-origin data leak). **Test**: User-Agent Sniffing checks Chrome version ≥ 149.0.7827.53; out-of-date users get a banner suggesting update. GameZipper itself not directly vulnerable, but any embedded ad iframe using older Chrome puts user credentials at risk. Source: https://securityweek.com/chrome-149-patches-429-vulnerabilities + https://cvefeed.io/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-11084

### 10.3 Safari 26.5 20 WebKit CVEs (CVE-2026-28905–28971) + WebRTC CVE-2026-28944
- [B-018] **[P0]** Safari 26.5+ required for production — 20 WebKit CVEs (memory corruption, OOB read, use-after-free) all fixed in Safari 26.5 / iOS 26.5 / iPadOS 26.5 / macOS Tahoe 26.5. **Test**: UA sniff Safari version ≥ 26.5; out-of-date iOS users shown update banner. CVE-2026-28947 is process crash via malicious web content. WebRTC CVE-2026-28944 patched in same release. Source: https://news.lavx.hu/article/safari-26-5-patches-20-webkit-flaws-and-a-webrtc-crash-bug-what-ios-ipados-and-macos-developers-need-to-know + https://cvefeed.io/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-28847

### 10.4 Safari 26.5 New Web Platform Features
- [B-019] **[P1]** Safari 26.5 `:open` pseudo-class — game UI using `<details>`, `<select>`, `<dialog>`, or `<input>` (date/color picker) can style their open state via `select:open`, `details:open`, `dialog:open` with progressive enhancement (no-op on older browsers). **Test**: verify CSS like `select:open { border-color: skyblue }` applies when dropdown expands on Safari 26.5+. Source: https://webkit.org/blog/17938/webkit-features-for-safari-26-5
- [B-020] **[P1]** Safari 26.5 `Origin` API — `window.origin` now returns a structured `Origin` object (not just a string), with `.scheme`, `.host`, `.port` properties. **Test**: `if (window.origin && typeof window.origin === 'object' && 'host' in window.origin) { ... }` detection; fallback to `window.location.origin` string on pre-26.5 Safari. Source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/safari-release-notes/safari-26_5-release-notes
- [B-021] **[P1]** Safari 26.5 `ToggleEvent.source` — popover invoker attribution works. Game pages using popover-based menus/settings (e.g. "How to play" popover) can identify which button opened the popover. **Test**: bind popover via `popoverTargetElement` or `popovertarget` attribute, verify `toggleEvent.source` returns the invoker element. Source: https://webkit.org/blog/17938/webkit-features-for-safari-26-5

### 10.5 Chrome I/O 2026 — HTML-in-Canvas API
- [G-017] **[P1]** HTML-in-Canvas API accessibility — when a game uses WebGL/WebGPU/Canvas2D for rendering and embeds HTML UI inside the canvas (Chrome 149+ I/O 2026 feature), the HTML elements remain in the accessibility tree (NVDA/VoiceOver/TalkBack) and accept focus/keyboard input. **Test**: if any game adopts HTML-in-Canvas pattern, run Playwright `page.accessibility.snapshot()` to verify embedded HTML buttons are reachable. Fallback path required for pre-Chrome 149. Source: https://webgpu.com (Google I/O 2026 announcement by Thomas Nattestad)

### 10.6 Safari First-Draw Shader Stall (Unity WebGL pattern)
- [G-018] **[P1]** First-draw shader warmup — games using WebGL/Three.js/Babylon.js must pre-render (instantiate + draw at least one frame during splash) all major shader programs before gameplay starts. Safari's `webglPrepareUniformLocationsBeforeFirstDraw` is synchronous main-thread (~3ms per uniform lookup × 200 uniforms = ~3s freeze). **Test**: on iOS Safari, play any 3D game (e.g. stacker-3d, sushi-stack-3d) for 60s; record timeline; assert no >500ms main-thread stall in first 5s of gameplay. **Mitigation**: render all prefabs into an offscreen 1×1 canvas during splash. Source: https://richardfu.net/unity-webgl-safari-hang-shader-warmup (May 2026 post-mortem)

### 10.7 Origin API Tracking-Integrity
- [W-037] **[P2]** Origin API opt-in / fallback — analytics and auth code paths that check origin must handle both structured Origin object (Safari 26.5+) and string (legacy). **Test**: in any gz-analytics.js, gz_aa, gz_ab localStorage archive path, ensure `String(window.location.origin || (window.origin && window.origin.toString()) || 'unknown')` is the call pattern; no `window.origin.host` access without `typeof window.origin === 'object'` check. Source: https://webkit.org/blog/17938/webkit-features-for-safari-26-5

### 10.8 Chrome 149 429-Patch Record — Untrusted Input Validation
- [W-038] **[P1]** No untrusted-input handling that mirrors Chrome 149 CVE pattern — game-data.js / GAMES array entries come from a controlled build pipeline (no runtime user input), but any code that takes query string or localStorage and writes to innerHTML, eval, or `new Function()` is exposed. **Test**: `grep -rn "innerHTML\s*=\|eval(\|new Function(" js/ gamezipper.com/*/index.html | grep -v 'innerHTML = ""'` — must return only safe patterns. Source: https://pcworld.com/article/3158038/chrome-149-fixes-429-security-flaws-the-most-ever-in-one-update.html (majority of 429 patches are use-after-free / insufficient untrusted input validation)
