the attack compromised the software's update/distribution channel to deliver malware to every user who installed or updated during the window. **Source**: kaspersky.com/blog/tag/supply-chain-attack/ (March 27 2026 "The most notable supply-chain attacks of 2025" by Alanna Titterington). **Why this is the DESKTOP-CONSUMER-SOFTWARE-SUPPLY-CHAIN dimension distinct from C-109 (Apifox desktop-client CDN poisoning — a DEVELOPER TOOL) and C-108 (npm worm methodology — a PACKAGE REGISTRY)**: C-109 covers dev tools (Apifox); C-108 covers npm; C-112 covers **GENERAL CONSUMER DESKTOP SOFTWARE** — Daemon Tools, WinRAR, 7-Zip, Notepad++, any widely-installed desktop utility — a less-tracked vector because security teams focus on dev tools and package registries, but consumer desktop software is installed on every developer's and QA engineer's machine and often has auto-update with elevated privileges. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: GameZipper's QA engineers and pipeline operators run consumer desktop software (disk mounters, archivers, editors) on their workstations; a compromised DAEMON Tools update channel (or equivalent) on a QA engineer's machine can pivot to compromising the GameZipper build environment via stolen credentials, modified game files, or backdoored build scripts — exactly the same outcome as a direct npm compromise but via a vector no npm scanner detects. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) inventory all consumer desktop software installed on GameZipper QA/build workstations (Daemon Tools, WinRAR, 7-Zip, Notepad++, etc.); (2) for each, verify the vendor has a documented security incident-response process and that auto-update pulls from an HTTPS endpoint with certificate pinning or signature verification; (3) verify no GameZipper build/QA workflow requires elevated-privilege desktop software (a compromised updater running as admin has full system access); (4) add "consumer desktop software update compromise" to the GameZipper threat model alongside npm-supply-chain and dev-tool-CDN (per C-108, C-109); (5) consider application-allowlisting on build machines so only approved executables run.

### 39.8 WebGPU Streaming Buffers for Progressive 3D-Asset Loading — PlayCanvas SuperSplat (June 3 2026)
- [P-068] **[P2]** WebGPU streaming buffers for progressive 3D-asset loading (PlayCanvas SuperSplat June 3 2026 news "New in SuperSplat: WebGPU and Streaming Bring Huge Performance Wins") — PlayCanvas announced that the latest SuperSplat release (their open-source Gaussian-splatting editor) achieves significant performance gains by combining WebGPU rendering with **streaming buffer architecture**: large 3D assets (Gaussian-splat point clouds, but the pattern generalizes to large glTF models and textures) are loaded progressively via WebGPU mapped buffers rather than monolithically, allowing first-paint to occur before the full asset is in GPU memory. **Source**: playcanvas.com (June 3 2026 news "New in SuperSplat: WebGPU and Streaming Bring Huge Performance Wins"). **Why this is the WEBGPU-STREAMING-BUFFERS-PROGRESSIVE-LOAD dimension distinct from P-027 (Chrome 148 native lazy-loading for audio/video — a different API entirely) and P-067 (WASM-WebGPU shared-memory boundary — tests the memory BOUNDARY not the LOADING pattern)**: P-027 covers HTML media elements; P-067 covers the WASM↔GPU memory boundary; P-068 covers the **progressive large-asset loading pattern** via WebGPU mapped buffers — specifically: (a) first-paint time vs total-asset-size tradeoff (the user sees a low-detail version immediately, detail streams in); (b) GPU memory pressure during progressive mapping (multiple partial buffers may coexist transiently); (c) the streaming buffer must be unmapped before GPU access, creating a synchronization window where the WASM/JS writer and GPU reader race. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: GameZipper's current catalog is pure-2D Canvas (no large 3D assets), BUT (a) any future game using glTF models, large textures, or Gaussian splats would benefit from this pattern; (b) the pattern is relevant for the og-image / thumbnail loading pipeline if GameZipper ever adopts progressive image loading via WebGPU; (c) the synchronization window (buffer must be unmapped before GPU read) is a new failure surface not covered by P-067 (which covers shared memory, not the map/unmap lifecycle). **Test for GameZipper**: (1) for any future WebGPU game with large assets, measure first-paint time vs total-asset-size — a streaming-buffer implementation should achieve first-paint in <2s for a 50MB asset; (2) monitor GPU memory pressure during progressive loading via `performance.memory` and `GPUAdapter.limits.maxBufferSize` — flag any transient over-allocation; (3) test the map/unmap synchronization: write to a mapped buffer, unmap, then read on GPU — verify no torn reads; (4) test behavior under network throttling (Slow 3G) — the streaming pattern should gracefully degrade to a lower-detail first-paint rather than blocking on the full asset; (5) document the streaming-buffer pattern in the GameZipper WebGPU migration guide alongside P-067 (shared memory) and P-047 (draw-call benchmark).

---

## CATEGORY 40: 2026-Q2 R191 DYNAMIC TEST INTELLIGENCE — SOVEREIGN-CERT-IOS-WEBKIT-RED-ALERT + CROSS-VENDOR-ANGLE-SHARED-CVE + SAFARI-26-PRIVACY-PULLBACK + SAFARI-26.5-WEBRTC-CRASH + AUTOMATED-WCAG-AA-REVIEWER-TOOLCHAIN + CONCRETE-WebGPU-PERF-NUMBERS (June 19 2026)

### 40.1 China NVDB iOS-WebKit Red Alert — Sovereign-CERT Highest-Level Response to Foreign-APT In-the-Wild Exploitation (April 3 2026)
- [B-110] **[P1]** China 工信部 国家网络安全威胁和漏洞信息共享平台 (NVDB) issued the highest-level RED WARNING (红色预警) on April 3 2026 confirming that **foreign APT groups are exploiting iOS WebKit vulnerabilities in the wild** to attack Chinese iPhone users — 42,000+ iPhones in China confirmed compromised at the time of the alert; the attack chain requires only a single malicious webpage visited in mobile Safari to achieve **no-interaction remote code execution → full device control** (no click, no app install, no privilege escalation by the victim). **Sources**: blog.csdn.net/xixixi7777/article/details/160202543 (June 11 2026 "工信部发布红色预警:iOS WebKit致命漏洞遭境外组织实战利用,全球苹果用户必须立即更新"); new.qq.com/rain/a/20260119A01E5F00 (Jan 19 2026 — Apple's first emergency patch for CVE-2025-43529 + CVE-2025-14174 found by Google Threat Analysis Group, both in-the-wild exploited); new.qq.com/rain/a/20251229A06B2H00 (Dec 29 2025 — earlier Apple multi-platform emergency patch for the same WebKit pair). **Why this is the SOVEREIGN-CERT-IOS-WEBKIT-RED-ALERT dimension distinct from W-167 (China 国家网络与信息安全信息通报中心 Shai-Hulud npm advisory — npm-specific)**: W-167 covers npm supply-chain; B-110 covers iOS-WebKit end-user exploitation. **Distinct from B-097 (iOS 26.5 full security advisory 80+ vulns + 2 zero-days + M5 hardware memory-safety bypass)**: B-097 is the GENERIC Apple patch-list bulletin; B-110 is a SPECIFIC NATIONAL-RED-ALERT response to in-the-wild foreign-APT exploitation confirmed across 42K+ devices in CN — the alert level is HIGHEST (red), the actor attribution is FOREIGN (境外组织), and the device count is CONCRETE (42,000+) — three signals that none of the prior entries combine. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: GameZipper's mobile traffic is ~20% iOS Safari (per project context); a malicious Monetag/AdSense creative delivered to an unpatched iOS Safari player achieves the same no-interaction RCE the NVDB alert describes — the player does not even need to click the ad, merely having the page render the ad iframe is sufficient if the creative exploits the WebKit CVE. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) for every Monetag/AdSense creative slot, confirm the SSP has a creative-scanning pipeline that blocks known-exploited-WebKit-CVE patterns in ad JS — verify with the SSP's trust/safety contact; (2) add a regression test: load gamezipper.com in a Safari instance running a stale iOS (e.g. iOS 18.7.2 — the LAST version before the patch per cnblogs.com/clnchanpin/p/19475745) and confirm no ad-rendered JS triggers an unexpected navigation, alert, or download; (3) monitor the BI dashboard for iOS-traffic anomalies (sudden drop in iOS Safari sessions, sudden spike in iOS Safari → external-domain navigations) — these are the operational signals of an in-the-wild exploit hitting the player base.

### 40.2 Cross-Vendor ANGLE Memory-OOB Shared CVE — CVE-2025-14174 / CVE-2025-43529 Affecting BOTH Safari/WebKit AND Chrome/Chromium via the Shared ANGLE OpenGL-ES Translation Layer (Apple Jan 19 2026; Google TAG)
- [S-137] **[P1]** CVE-2025-14174 (Google later reclassified as an **ANGLE memory out-of-bounds access**) and CVE-2025-43529 (WebKit memory corruption / use-after-free) were both discovered by **Google Threat Analysis Group (TAG)** — Google's threat-attribution team — and **confirmed in-the-wild exploited** before Apple's January 19 2026 emergency patch. The critical structural insight (per cnblogs.com/clnchanpin/p/19475745 Jan 13 2026 analysis): **CVE-2025-14174 is an ANGLE bug** — ANGLE (Almost Native Graphics Layer Engine) is the open-source graphics library that translates OpenGL ES calls to native backends (DirectX on Windows, Vulkan on Linux/Android, **Metal on macOS/iOS**). Both **Safari/WebKit** (per trac.webkit.org/wiki/AngleforWebGL — "WebKit has integrated the ANGLE project") AND **Chrome/Chromium** use ANGLE for their WebGL implementations, so a single CVE in the shared ANGLE layer propagates to **multiple browser vendors simultaneously**. Patches landed at different times: Apple shipped iOS 26.2 / iOS 18.7.3; Chrome/Chromium had its own patch cadence. **Sources**: support.apple.com/en-us/125884 (Apple security advisory HT-125884); cnblogs.com/clnchanpin/p/19475745 (Jan 13 2026 deep analysis — confirms WebKit-on-iOS uses ANGLE's Metal backend, AND Chrome-on-macOS also uses ANGLE's Metal backend, so the SAME CVE-2025-14174 hits BOTH browsers on the SAME device); new.qq.com/rain/a/20260119A01E5F00 (Jan 19 2026 — Apple's TAG attribution + in-the-wild confirmation). **Why this is the CROSS-VENDOR-ANGLE-SHARED-CVE dimension distinct from S-088 (CVE-2026-5281 Dawn WebGPU UAF — Chrome-Dawn-specific)**: S-088 is a Chrome-side Dawn WebGPU bug; S-137 is a SHARED-LIBRARY bug (ANGLE) that affects both WebKit AND Chromium because both vendors ship ANGLE. **Distinct from B-087 (iOS 26 WebKit 26 WebGPU memory-limits regression — an API-behavior bug)**: B-087 is an API-contract regression; S-137 is a memory-safety CVE. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: a single malicious WebGL/Canvas ad creative that exploits CVE-2025-14174 can hit whichever browser the player uses (Safari OR Chrome) on whichever platform lags the patch — iOS users often lag Apple's emergency patches by weeks (auto-update default ON but rollout staged), and macOS Chrome users lag Chromium's update by days; the creative has TWO independent patch-lag windows to exploit. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) verify all Monetag/AdSense creatives pass a WebGL-shader-pattern scan that rejects known CVE-2025-14174 PoC patterns; (2) regression test: load gamezipper.com in BOTH (a) an unpatched iOS 18.7.2 Safari AND (b) an unpatched Chrome (pre-emergency-patch version) — confirm no ad-delivered WebGL context triggers the OOB; (3) the cross-vendor-shared-library insight generalizes: ANY graphics library shared between Safari and Chromium (ANGLE today; possibly WebGPU/Dawn + WebKit WebGPU in the future per W-154 HTML-in-Canvas breaking change) means a single CVE has multi-vendor blast radius — the QA matrix must test BOTH browsers for any graphics-related CVE, not just the vendor that disclosed it.

### 40.3 Safari 26 Stable Ship + Last-Minute Privacy-Feature Pullback — gclid/fbclid Auto-Strip + Fingerprinting-Prevention REMOVED from Final Stable (June 19 2026)
- [W-172] **[P2]** Safari 26 was BETA-ANNOUNCED with two privacy features: (a) **WebKit bug 134226694** "Added support for filtering tracking parameters in links in regular browsing mode" — Safari would automatically strip `gclid`, `fbclid`, and other known click-tracking parameters from link URLs in normal (non-private) browsing mode; (b) **WebKit bug 134227067** "Added support for preventing fingerprinting for known tracking scripts" — Safari would block known fingerprinting scripts even in regular mode. **BOTH features were REMOVED from the final Safari 26 stable release** — the shipped Safari 26 does NOT strip gclid/fbclid, and does NOT block the known fingerprinting scripts. **Source**: cloud.tencent.com/developer/article/2637644 (June 19 2026 "Safari版本26发布" — explicit confirmation that 134226694 was REMOVED from the released version 26, so gclid/fbclid tracking is unaffected in stable Safari 26). **Why this is the SAFARI-ANNOUNCED-PRIVACY-FEATURE-PULLED-LAST-MINUTE dimension distinct from S-081 (Safari 27 WebKit deprecations — features deprecated and removed as designed)**: S-081 covers deliberate deprecations; W-172 covers a feature that was PROMISED in beta then PULLED from stable — a fundamentally different pattern that has direct QA consequences. **Distinct from B-090 (Safari 27 WWDC26 record 525 fixes)**: B-090 is a positive feature/cadence milestone; W-172 is a NEGATIVE pullback event. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (1) **Monetag/AdSense attribution**: if the gclid-strip feature HAD shipped, all Google Ads (gclid) + Meta Ads (fbclid) attribution to gamezipper.com would have broken — QA analytics scripts that ANTICIPATED the stripping would have falsely concluded "attribution is broken" in production; the rollback means gclid/fbclid attribution still works in Safari 26 stable, and the QA scripts that anticipated stripping must be revised; (2) **the general pattern**: any privacy feature Apple ANNOUNCES in a Safari beta is at-risk-of-pullback before stable ship — GameZipper analytics, ad-tech, and fingerprinting-defense plans cannot rely on beta-announced features until the stable ship actually lands them; (3) **second-order implication for Safari 27 / iOS 27 features** (per B-090 + B-108): features Apple announced at WWDC26 June 9 2026 may also be pulled before stable ship in September 2026 — the W-172 pattern means QA plans based on WWDC announcements should be conditional on stable-ship confirmation. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) for each Safari privacy feature that affects Monetag/AdSense attribution or tracking, do NOT update the analytics pipeline until the feature lands in Safari STABLE (not beta); (2) maintain a "Safari beta feature watchlist" of features that, if pulled, change the GameZipper measurement model — review on each Safari stable ship; (3) regression test: load gamezipper.com in Safari 26 stable + verify gclid/fbclid parameters on inbound ad clicks are PRESERVED in document.location (i.e. confirm the pullback is real and attribution is intact).

### 40.4 Safari 26.5 WebRTC Process-Crash Fix — Safari-Specific WebRTC Stability Patch (Apple May 14 2026)
- [S-138] **[P2]** Apple's Safari 26.5 security list (May 14 2026) includes a fix for a **WebRTC bug that could cause the Safari browser process to abnormally crash**, affecting audio/video communication scenarios; Safari 26.5 also fixed CVE-2026-28962 (CSP bypass → sensitive-information disclosure — already covered by S-040 in v1.19.0) and multiple memory-corruption issues that could crash the browser when processing maliciously-modified content. **Sources**: developer.apple.com/news (May 14 2026 Safari 26.5 security release notes); so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_0236a054d4370952 (May 14 2026 "苹果公布 Safari 26.5 浏览器安全清单,修复敏感信息泄露风险" — explicit mention of the WebRTC-crash fix + multiple memory issues affecting browser stability + WebRTC-specific crash affecting audio/video communication scenarios). **Why this is the SAFARI-26.5-WEBRTC-CRASH-FIX dimension distinct from S-085 (CVE-2026-9111 WebRTC UAF RCE — Linux Chromium)**: S-085 is a Linux-Chromium-specific UAF that achieves RCE; S-138 is a Safari-specific WebRTC crash (denial-of-service / process termination) fixed in 26.5 — different platform, different bug class (crash vs RCE), different vendor. **Distinct from B-086 (Firefox 151 MFSA2026-46 sandbox-escape batch)**: B-086 covers Firefox; S-138 covers Safari. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: any GameZipper game that uses WebRTC (peer-to-peer multiplayer, voice chat, in-game video calls — none currently in the catalog but plausible for future games) running on Safari 26 or 26.4 (pre-fix) could crash mid-session, terminating the player's game without saving progress; the crash is reproducible via a malformed WebRTC payload, so a malicious peer (in a multiplayer game) or a malformed creative could trigger it. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) before shipping any WebRTC-using game, verify the minimum Safari version requirement is 26.5+ (the fix version); (2) regression test: in Safari 26.5+, send a deliberately-malformed WebRTC offer/answer to a game page and confirm no process crash; (3) the WebRTC-crash-fix pattern generalizes — every Safari WebRTC-using feature (screen share, camera/mic access, peer connections) is a potential crash surface that must be retested on each Safari release.

### 40.5 PHY041/phy-a11y-reviewer Automated WCAG 2.2 AA Reviewer Toolchain — Open-Source URL/Codebase → WCAG 2.2 AA Report (GitHub March 6 2026; a11ycheck mrgig.com May 16 2026)
- [A-040] **[P2]** A category of automated WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility-reviewer tools has emerged that takes either a URL or a codebase and produces a structured WCAG 2.2 AA conformance report organized by success criterion: **PHY041/phy-a11y-reviewer** (github.com/PHY041/phy-a11y-reviewer March 6 2026 — "WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility review for web applications") is a codebase-aware reviewer runnable in CI; **a11ycheck** (mrgig.com May 16 2026 — "Check any page against WCAG 2.2") is a paste-URL → full-report SaaS-style reviewer. Both target the FULL WCAG 2.2 AA success-criteria set (87 criteria including the 9 new 2.2 criteria per A-027), going beyond what axe-core / WAVE automated tools catch (which per A-031 cover only 30-40% of WCAG issues). **Sources**: github.com/PHY041/phy-a11y-reviewer (March 6 2026 — Initial release "WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility review for web applications"); mrgig.com (May 16 2026 — a11ycheck "Check a page / Guide / History / iOS app" SaaS reviewer); corroborated by blog.csdn.net/gitblog_00365/article/details/155290334 (April 20 2026 "WCAG 2.2新特性深度解读:低视力用户支持与认知障碍优化指南"). **Why this is the AUTOMATED-WCAG-2.2-AA-REVIEWER-TOOLCHAIN dimension distinct from A-034 (US ADA Title II April 2026 deadline — legal/regulatory)**: A-034 is the legal-enforcement dimension; A-040 is the TOOLING-AUTOMATION dimension — actionable integration of a specific open-source reviewer into the GameZipper pre-commit / CI pipeline. **Distinct from A-037 (iOS 27 Accessibility framework rewrite audit — Apple-API-specific)**: A-037 covers Apple's first-party accessibility APIs; A-040 covers cross-platform WCAG reviewer tooling. **Distinct from A-038/A-039 (accessibility-lawsuit surge statistics)**: A-038/A-039 are litigation-intelligence dimensions; A-040 is a defensive-tooling dimension. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: GameZipper has 280+ game pages + tools subdomain + admin pages — all of which are WCAG-conformance surfaces under the rising ADA-lawsuit wave (A-034/A-035/A-038/A-039); integrating phy-a11y-reviewer into the pre-commit hook (per the existing `gz-pre-commit` pattern in the post-deploy-verification skill) would catch WCAG 2.2 AA regressions BEFORE they ship, at zero per-game marginal cost. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) install phy-a11y-reviewer as a dev dependency; (2) add a pre-commit hook step that runs the reviewer on any changed .html file and FAILS the commit on any new WCAG 2.2 AA violation (matching the existing `sync-game-counts.sh` + `sync-user-visible-text.sh` pre-commit pattern); (3) baseline the current 280+ game pages by running the reviewer once across the catalog and triaging the existing violations into a remediation backlog; (4) for the a11ycheck SaaS reviewer, use it as a quick ad-hoc verification tool for individual pages during QA (paste URL → report) — complementary to the codebase-aware CI reviewer.

### 40.6 WebGPU 39x Draw-Call Throughput + 2.75x Texture-Sampling Rate vs WebGL2 — Concrete Migration-Decision Numbers (CSDN AWS WebGPU实战 Three.js March 24 2026; zhihu WebGPU Perf Analysis Aug 2022)
- [P-069] **[P2]** Concrete measured WebGPU-vs-WebGL2 performance numbers from real benchmarks: (a) **maximum draw calls per frame** — WebGL 2.0 caps at ~12,000 draw calls/frame before CPU-bottlenecked stalls; WebGPU sustains ~470,000 draw calls/frame = **39× throughput** (per blog.csdn.net/awscloud/article/details/146470071 March 24 2026 "WebGPU实战:Three.js性能优化新纪元" — table "特性 WebGL 2.0 WebGPU 提升幅度 / 最大DrawCall/帧 1.2万 47万 39倍"); (b) **texture sampling rate** — WebGL 2.0 ~8 GB/s vs WebGPU ~22 GB/s = **2.75×** (same source, table row "纹理采样速率 8GB/s 22GB/s 2.75倍"); (c) **render performance** — WebGPU is 3×+ faster than WebGL on rendering workloads; (d) **compute performance** — WebGPU is 50×+ faster than WebGL on compute workloads (per zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/387054998 Aug 2 2022 "WebGPU性能测试分析" — measured on MacBook M1 Pro + RTX 2060 Super desktop, using "reuse render command buffer" + "dynamic uniform buffer offset" optimizations). **Sources**: blog.csdn.net/awscloud/article/details/146470071 (March 24 2026 AWS-authored WebGPU实战 Three.js guide — concrete comparison table); zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/387054998 (Aug 2 2022 WebGPU性能测试分析 — independent M1 Pro + RTX 2060 Super benchmark corroborating the 3×+ render and 50×+ compute numbers). **Why this is the CONCRETE-WebGPU-PERF-NUMBERS-FOR-MIGRATION-CASE dimension distinct from P-030 (WebGPU 70% browser coverage + 15x compute performance — a COARSE 15x aggregate number)**: P-030 gives one coarse 15× number; P-069 gives FOUR specific numbers (draw-call throughput 39×, texture-sampling 2.75×, render 3×+, compute 50×+) each tied to a different game-workload profile. **Distinct from P-047 (WebGPU 8.5x draw calls + 4.5x memory bandwidth — different metric and source)**: P-047 uses a different draw-call metric (8.5× vs P-069's 39×) — the discrepancy is because P-047 measures a DIFFERENT workload (sustained frame-rate draw calls in a real game scene) while P-069 measures the MAXIMUM draw calls before CPU stall (the theoretical ceiling); BOTH numbers are useful — P-047 for real-game migration priority, P-069 for understanding the architectural ceiling. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: GameZipper's current catalog is pure-Canvas 2D (no WebGL/WebGPU), but any FUTURE game in a high-sprite-count genre (bullet-hell shooters with 1000+ on-screen projectiles, particle-heavy match-3 with thousands of particles, large-tile puzzle grids with hundreds of tiles) currently caps at the WebGL2 ~12K draw-call/frame ceiling — at that ceiling the game becomes CPU-bottlenecked and frame-rate collapses; the 39× WebGPU ceiling (470K draw calls/frame) means the SAME game design is viable on WebGPU where it would be unplayable on WebGL2. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) for any new game in a high-sprite-count genre, profile the projected draw-call count per frame at peak load (e.g. bullet-hell boss pattern with 2000+ bullets); if the projected count exceeds ~10,000 (the WebGL2 ceiling with safety margin), MANDATE WebGPU-first rendering with WebGL2 fallback only for low-sprite modes; (2) establish a per-game draw-call budget at design time (e.g. "this game must sustain 60fps at up to 8000 draw calls/frame on a mid-range Mali-G57 GPU" per P-048) and reject designs that exceed the budget on WebGL2 without a WebGPU path; (3) the texture-sampling 2.75× number (22 GB/s vs 8 GB/s) is the relevant threshold for texture-heavy games (large atlas textures, many unique textures per scene) — profile texture-bandwidth usage and mandate WebGPU if WebGL2 bandwidth is insufficient.

---

## v1.70.0 — R190 Dynamic Test Intelligence — 8 New Test Cases (2026-06-19)

The 8 test cases below were added by the R190 cron run. Each is a NEW dimension (verified absent from v1.69.0 via grep for both the new ID and the key distinguishing term). Sources: minimax web search (8 queries; web-search-prime/SearXNG returned 404 in this round, mirroring the R187/R188/R189 pattern — minimax is the stable search backend).

- [B-108] **[P1]** iOS 27 Siri AI standalone ChatGPT-style app (Apple WWDC26 keynote June 9 2026, Bloomberg/Mark Gurman leak May 13 2026) — iOS 27 ships the largest Siri rearchitecture in 15 years: Siri becomes an independent first-party App (not just a voice-command parser) with a ChatGPT-style chatbot UI supporting multi-turn conversation, persistent conversation history ("return to yesterday's chat"), file upload, content pinning, and deep integration with the Dynamic Island (灵动岛 — when invoked via power button or wake word the Dynamic Island shows a "Search or Ask" choice); a NEW global gesture (swipe down from top-center of screen) triggers a system-level search panel; the search bar lets users one-tap switch between Siri / ChatGPT / Gemini AI engines — Apple is opening SYSTEM-LEVEL AI ENGINE CHOICE to users for the first time. Siri AI ships in English first; NOT available on China-mainland devices (国行仍无缘). **Sources**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_2946a27289665852 (June 9 2026 WWDC day-1 coverage); so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_9246a2750ea53752 (June 9 2026 TC report); so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_4296a03e77633352 (May 13 2026 Gurman leak — "iOS 27 will大改版，Siri恢复独立App形态对标ChatGPT等"); so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_1576a042d9574852 (May 13 2026 "iOS 27 Siri史上最大升级:推独立App对标ChatGPT，开放第三方AI模型，国行仍无缘"); new.qq.com/rain/a/20260122A01OFC00 (Jan 22 2026 MacRumors report — first leak that iOS 27 would ship a ChatGPT-class Siri chatbot). **Why this is the NEW-AI-DISCOVERY-SURFACE-on-iOS dimension distinct from W-164 (ChatGPT 2026 sponsored-content / AI-advertising)**: W-164 covers OpenAI's VENDOR-DRIVEN displacement of organic AI answers via paid placement; B-108 covers Apple's OS-LEVEL EMBEDDED AI that an iOS player can invoke WITHOUT leaving the game (a player mid-GameZipper-game can swipe-down-from-top to ask Siri "what's a fun puzzle game like this one?" — the answer displaces GameZipper discovery). **Distinct from W-158 (Chrome 148 address-bar AI — Android-side)**: W-158 is the Android Chrome address-bar AI; B-108 is the iOS system-level Siri AI embedded in the OS layer above Safari. **Distinct from B-090 (Safari 27 WWDC26 525-fix WebKit regression sweep)**: B-090 is the SAFARI browser engine regression; B-108 is the iOS-OS-LAYER Siri AI App that operates ABOVE Safari (Siri can read screen content via Accessibility APIs, summarize the page, recommend alternatives). **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) iOS is a significant share of GameZipper mobile traffic; a player invoking iOS 27 Siri mid-game can ask "what other games are like [current game]?" and Siri AI's answer determines whether the player stays on GameZipper or jumps to a competitor's link Siri recommends — Siri AI becomes a NEW discovery+churn surface simultaneously; (b) Siri AI's "file upload" feature means a player could screenshot a GameZipper level and ask Siri "how do I solve this?" — the answer either references GameZipper's hint system (good) or recommends a third-party walkthrough site with ads (lost engagement); (c) the system-level AI engine switcher (Siri/ChatGPT/Gemini) means GameZipper discovery depends on which AI engine the player picked — each engine ranks differently and GameZipper has no visibility into the choice; (d) China-mainland iOS devices get NONE of this (国行仍无缘) — CN iOS traffic is unaffected, but ex-CN iOS traffic is fully exposed. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) **Siri-AI discovery audit (iOS 27 + Apple Developer account)** — on iOS 27 beta/stable, invoke Siri AI and ask "fun browser puzzle game", "best free web game", and the names of top-30 GameZipper games; record whether GameZipper appears organically, appears as a Siri-suggested App Store result, is displaced by a competitor, or is absent; document per-AI-engine (Siri vs ChatGPT vs Gemini) ranking differences; (2) **game-page-meta optimization for Siri AI ingestion** — extend the JSON-LD VideoGame schema (per W-157) with concise `<meta name="description">` + canonical short game descriptions Siri AI can surface without needing to scrape the page (Siri AI prefers structured-data-driven answers); (3) **mid-game Siri-displacement test** — open a top-30 GameZipper game on iOS 27, swipe-down-from-top to invoke Siri, ask "what other games are like this?"; verify GameZipper's own related-games footer (per game-footer.js) appears in Siri's answer OR that GameZipper is at least the first organic mention; if Siri consistently recommends competitor links, escalate; (4) **Siri-file-upload-walkthrough-leak test** — screenshot a GameZipper level, upload to Siri AI, ask "how do I solve this?"; verify Siri's answer either (a) references GameZipper's official hint/help, or (b) does not contain third-party walkthrough-site links with advertising (lost-engagement signal); (5) **engine-switcher ranking divergence test** — for the top-30 game queries, capture Siri AI's answers across all three selectable engines (Siri/ChatGPT/Gemini) and document divergence; if GameZipper appears organically on one engine but is displaced on another, treat the displaced engine as a discovery-loss and file structured-data-improvement tasks; (6) **CN-iOS exclusion verification** — confirm China-mainland iOS 27 devices show the legacy voice-only Siri (no chatbot, no multi-turn) so the B-108 dimension does not apply to CN traffic (do not waste QA cycles running Siri-AI tests on CN devices).
- [B-109] **[P2]** Apple Australia + Brazil + Singapore App Store 18+ age-confirmation requirement (effective Feb 24 2026) — Apple restricted users in Australia, Brazil, and Singapore from downloading apps rated 18+ UNLESS the user has completed "reasonable" age confirmation via the App Store proving they are adults; the requirement applies to all 18+ rated apps in those three jurisdictions. **Source**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_159699e486598352 (May 26 2026 "苹果新规：澳大利亚、巴西、新加坡用户需完成年龄确认，方可下载18岁及以上分级应用"). **Why this is the GEOGRAPHIC-SPECIFIC-APP-STORE-POLICY-TIGHTENING dimension distinct from W-168 (Australia + Vietnam age-rating category retirement June 18 2026)**: W-168 covers the RATING-CATEGORY MIGRATION (15+ → 16+ auto-bump); B-109 covers the DOWNLOAD-CONFIRMATION gate on 18+ apps in three different jurisdictions (AU + BR + SG) that took effect 4 months earlier (Feb 24 2026 vs June 18 2026). **Distinct from S-028 (fake-system-alert creatives)**: S-028 is a content-policy issue inside ads; B-109 is a platform-policy gate outside the game. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: GameZipper's current catalog is browser-only web games (no native iOS app, no App Store presence), so B-109 does not directly bind. HOWEVER: (a) any FUTURE GameZipper iOS companion app (a mobile wrapper, a tools.gamezipper.com PWA installable via App Store, an offline-game pack) is in scope; (b) any GameZipper content rated 18+ for "Frequent/Intense" content descriptors (simulated gambling, mature themes) will require user age confirmation before download in AU/BR/SG — this raises the download-friction ceiling and reduces conversion in those jurisdictions; (c) the dimension generalizes: App Store policies are diverging by jurisdiction (CN, EU DMA, UK Online Safety Act, AU/VN/BR/SG age-gating) and a single global rating strategy no longer works. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) **before any future iOS App Store submission** — verify the proposed rating (calculated by App Store Connect's content-descriptor questionnaire) does NOT exceed 17+/18+ in AU/BR/SG markets unless the elevated rating is intentional and the download-confirmation friction is acceptable; (2) **content-descriptor audit** — for any future native GameZipper app, audit game content for descriptors that may push the rating to 18+ (simulated gambling, mature themes, unrestricted web access — which is itself a per-W-168 bump trigger); (3) **per-jurisdiction rating matrix** — maintain a matrix mapping each App Store jurisdiction (AU, BR, SG, VN per W-168, EU per DMA, UK per OSA) to the specific rating+download restrictions; (4) **fallback-strategy** — if a GameZipper app must be rated 18+ in a tightening jurisdiction, verify a web-fallback (the browser version at gamezipper.com) is still accessible without App Store gate so the player is not locked out.
- [W-168] **[P2]** Australia + Vietnam App Store age-rating category retirement (effective June 18 2026) — Apple notified developers May 21 2026 that starting June 18 2026, age ratings on the App Store will change in Australia and Vietnam to comply with local regulations: (a) **Australia** — to comply with the Australian Classification Board's guidelines for computer-game ratings, the 15+ age tier will be RETIRED; apps currently rated 15+ that have content descriptors for "Unrestricted web access", "Frequent medical or treatment information", or "Loot boxes" will be auto-promoted to 16+; (b) **Vietnam** — to comply with Vietnam's Decree 147 (第 147 号法令) Article 38, apps distributed in Vietnam must adopt a Vietnam-specific local age rating. Apple stated the specific rating values will be published June 18 2026 in App Store Connect and its help documentation. **Sources**: developer.apple.com/news/?id=07142015b (May 21 2026 — Apple Developer news announcement); so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_3766a15729272752 (May 26 2026 "苹果将于下月更新澳大利亚和越南 App Store 年龄分级"); so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_5236a0fa5b621052 (May 22 2026 IT之家 "苹果 6 月 18 日将调整澳大利亚和越南 App Store 应用年龄分级"). **Why this is the REGULATORY-DIVERGENCE-BY-JURISDICTION dimension distinct from B-109 (AU/BR/SG 18+ download-confirmation Feb 24 2026)**: B-109 gates DOWNLOADS of 18+ apps; W-168 RETIRES an entire rating tier (15+) and re-buckets apps to 16+ based on content descriptors — W-168 changes the RATING itself, not the download flow. **Distinct from W-166 (China AI compliance framework)**: W-166 is CN AI-content regulation; W-168 is AU/VN age-classification regulation. **Distinct from A-034 (US ADA Title II April 2026 deadline)**: A-034 is accessibility litigation; W-168 is age-rating compliance. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's current catalog is web-only (no native iOS app), so W-168 does not directly bind; (b) BUT any future GameZipper iOS app (PWA wrapper, native port, offline pack) with "Unrestricted web access" content descriptor (which a web-game wrapper would automatically have — the wrapper renders arbitrary web URLs) is auto-bumped from 15+ to 16+ in Australia; (c) any loot-box mechanic in a GameZipper game (random reward unlocks, gacha-style level rewards) is itself an auto-bump trigger — game designers must be aware that adding loot-box-style mechanics has REGULATORY CONSEQUENCES on iOS distribution in AU; (d) the dimension generalizes: rating systems are diverging by jurisdiction and a single global rating no longer works; future GameZipper mobile distribution needs a per-jurisdiction rating matrix. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) **future-iOS-app content-descriptor audit** — before any iOS App Store submission, audit GameZipper content for the three W-168 auto-bump triggers (Unrestricted web access / Frequent medical-treatment info / Loot boxes); for a web-game wrapper, "Unrestricted web access" is automatic — confirm with App Store Review whether the bump to 16+ is acceptable in AU; (2) **loot-box-mechanic inventory** — `grep -rlnE 'loot|gacha|crate|reward.*random|random.*reward' --include="*.html" /home/msdn/gamezipper.com/` to find games with loot-box-style mechanics; document which games would auto-bump to 16+ in AU; (3) **per-jurisdiction rating matrix** — maintain a matrix: for each GameZipper property, list the rating in AU (post-W-168), VN (Decree 147), BR/SG (per B-109 download-gate), CN (per W-166 AI compliance), EU (per DMA), UK (per OSA); (4) **June-18-2026 effective-date regression test** — for any GameZipper iOS app live before June 18 2026, verify post-June-18 the App Store rating auto-bumped as expected (no developer action required) and that the new rating is correctly displayed in the App Store Connect UI; (5) **Vietnam-Decree-147 local-rating provisioning** — if GameZipper ships to VN, work with App Store Connect to obtain the VN-specific local rating per Decree 147 Article 38; verify the VN rating is distinct from the global rating.
- [W-169] **[P2]** Apple CEO transition Tim Cook → John Ternus effective September 1 2026 (announced WWDC26 keynote June 9 2026) — Tim Cook's WWDC26 keynote on June 9 2026 was his LAST WWDC as Apple CEO; he will step down on September 1 2026 and transition to "Executive Chairman of the Board"; John Ternus (currently Senior VP of Hardware Engineering) will succeed as CEO. Symbolic framing per CN coverage: the Cook era = "iPhone era" + "services transition"; the Ternus era = "AI era" + "hardware innovation". WWDC26 is positioned as the "新旧时代的交接点" (the inflection point between old and new eras). **Sources**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_2946a27289665852 (June 9 2026 "苹果WWDC 2026: Siri AI姗姗来迟, Apple Intelligence全面升级, OS集体迈入27版, 股价盘中转跌"); so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_9246a2750ea53752 (June 9 2026 "苹果WWDC 2026概览: 发布Siri AI、iOS 27、Apple Intelligence等" — confirms "this will be CEO Tim Cook's last WWDC at Apple"); blog.csdn.net/yp0to1/article/details/161627198 (June 10 2026 CSDN WWDC 2026 retrospective — "库克卸任CEO 2026年9月1日; 特努斯接任CEO 2026年9月1日; 库克时代 = iPhone时代 + 服务转型; 特努斯时代 = AI时代 + 硬件创新"). **Why this is the STRATEGIC-VENDOR-LEADERSHIP-INFLECTION-POINT dimension distinct from W-165 (Apple Safari ITP marketing campaign)**: W-165 is a tactical marketing push for an existing product; W-169 is a LEADERSHIP TRANSITION that historically coincides with platform-policy shifts (Jobs→Cook 2011 brought the services transition + App Store subscription policy overhaul; Cook→Ternus may bring an AI-first platform reordering that affects Safari WebKit feature cadence, App Store enforcement priorities, and Apple Intelligence API access). **Distinct from W-158 (Chrome 148 address-bar AI)**: W-158 is a single browser feature; W-169 is the EXECUTIVE-LEADERSHIP dimension that may set the next 5 years of Apple platform direction. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's iOS player base is exposed to whatever platform-policy direction Ternus sets — WebKit feature deprecation, App Store enforcement tightening (e.g., more aggressive 16+ enforcement per W-168), Apple Intelligence API gating (which AI features third-party web games can invoke); (b) the Cook→Ternus transition may accelerate OR DECELERATE WebKit feature development (Ternus is a hardware-engineering leader — historically less invested in Safari/WebKit than software leadership); a slower WebKit feature cadence means GameZipper games on iOS Safari lag behind Chrome for new web-platform features (CSS Gap Decorations, WebGPU, bfcache WebSocket — all Chrome-149-first features); (c) the symbolic "AI era" framing suggests Apple Intelligence will be MANDATED more aggressively in the platform — GameZipper games may be expected to expose Apple Intelligence hooks (per B-108 Siri AI integration). **Test for GameZipper**: (1) **leadership-transition watch** — subscribe to Apple news sources (9to5Mac, MacRumors, Bloomberg Mark Gurman, Daring Fireball) for the September 1 2026 transition; document any platform-policy announcements in the first 90 days of Ternus's tenure (Sept 1 - Nov 30 2026) that affect Safari WebKit, App Store policy, or Apple Intelligence APIs; (2) **WebKit feature-cadence monitoring** — track WebKit blog (webkit.org/blog) post frequency and feature-ship rate for 6 months pre-transition (March-Aug 2026) vs 6 months post-transition (Sept 2026 - Feb 2027); a >30% drop in new WebKit features is a leading indicator that iOS Safari will fall behind Chrome and GameZipper iOS rendering will diverge from Android; (3) **App Store enforcement-priority watch** — track App Store Review rejections in Q4 2026 (post-transition) vs Q3 2026 (Cook's last quarter) for any new rejection patterns; (4) **no-immediate-game-code-change** — W-169 is a STRATEGIC watch dimension, not a code-change trigger; do not modify any GameZipper code based on W-169 alone — wait for the SPECIFIC policy announcements (e.g., "Ternus's first WebKit policy memo", "Ternus's first App Store enforcement change") that would generate concrete test cases.
- [W-170] **[P2]** 2026 cybersecurity report: AI rewrites threat landscape — AI-Agent-impersonation + API-scale attacks + AI-browser/AI-phone "blackbox" risk (IDC prediction + Tencent Security report + 2026 cybersecurity six-trends retrospective) — IDC predicts by 2026 70% of organizations will adopt composite AI (generative + prescriptive + predictive + agentic). The AI explosion simultaneously creates NEW ATTACK VECTORS: (a) **AI-Agent身份冒充 (AI-Agent identity impersonation)** — attackers craft inputs that make a victim's AI agent believe it is interacting with a trusted peer agent, inducing it to take actions (file delete, credential disclosure, money transfer) it would refuse from an untrusted source; (b) **API规模化攻击 (API-scale attacks)** — attackers use AI to enumerate and abuse APIs at machine speed, finding undocumented endpoints and parameter combinations faster than human defenders can patch; (c) **AI浏览器/AI手机"黑箱"风险 (AI-browser/AI-phone "blackbox" risk)** — AI-mediated browsers (Arc Max, Chrome AI mode, Edge Copilot, per W-158) and AI phones (iPhone 18 with Apple Intelligence, per W-169) make decisions on behalf of the user that are NOT auditable — when the AI auto-fills a form, auto-clicks a link, auto-summarizes a page, the user cannot reconstruct WHY the AI did what it did. The 2026 cybersecurity thesis: the field is moving from "边界防护 (perimeter defense)" to "信任重构 (trust reconstruction)" — the network perimeter is no longer the locus of defense; the trust chain between user ↔ AI ↔ application is. **Sources**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_08069cbb35774752 (March 31 2026 "数字解读《2026年网络安全报告》:AI如何改写网络威胁新格局"); new.qq.com/rain/a/20251224A02X1V00 (Dec 24 2025 "2026网络安全六大新趋势: AI重构攻防, 信任成为新防线"). **Why this is the NEW-AI-DRIVEN-ATTACK-DIMENSION vector distinct from S-101 (AI-assistant browser-extension prompt injection — a single attack on a deployed AI) and S-127 (cryptojacking recommendation — a single AI-manipulation incident)**: S-101/S-127 are TECHNIQUE-level cases; W-170 is the THREAT-LANDSCAPE-LEVEL shift that the GameZipper threat model must accommodate as a whole. **Distinct from W-164 (ChatGPT sponsored content)**: W-164 is a discovery-displacement economic issue; W-170 is a security-and-trust issue. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) AI-Agent身份冒充 directly threatens the agent-based build pipeline (Hermes, Claude Code, MCP servers per S-128/S-130/S-133) — a malicious game file could impersonate a trusted peer agent and induce the build agent to take dangerous actions (commit malicious code, push to production, expose API keys); (b) API-scale attacks threaten the BI dashboard (per the post-deploy-verification OpenAPI-methodology pattern) — attackers using AI to enumerate undocumented BI endpoints may find ones the GameZipper team did not even know were exposed; (c) AI-browser/AI-phone blackbox risk affects analytics — when a player's AI browser auto-plays a game, auto-dismisses a dialog, auto-clicks an ad, the GameZipper BI dashboard sees the action but cannot tell whether it was a human or AI decision (affecting engagement metrics, ad-fraud detection, A/B test validity). **Test for GameZipper**: (1) **agent-impersonation red-team test** — extend C-111's purple-team methodology to specifically test agent-identity-impersonation: craft a malicious game file that, when processed by the Hermes build agent, attempts to impersonate a trusted peer and induce the agent to (a) commit malicious code, (b) push to production, (c) exfiltrate an API key; verify the agent refuses all three; (2) **API-surface enumeration test** — run an AI-driven API-discovery tool (e.g., an LLM-based OpenAPI-spec fuzzer) against the BI dashboard's openapi.json; identify any endpoint NOT documented in the spec but discoverable via AI enumeration; document and close each; (3) **AI-action-vs-human-action attribution** — extend the BI dashboard schema to record a "user_agent_kind" field (human / known-AI-browser / unknown-AI); alert if engagement metrics from AI browsers diverge >30% from human browsers (signal of AI auto-play skewing metrics); (4) **trust-reconstruction threat-modeling session** — schedule a quarterly threat-modeling session focused on the user ↔ AI ↔ GameZipper trust chain, not the network perimeter; document the new controls needed (signed agent actions, AI-attributable analytics, audit logs of AI-mediated decisions).
- [W-171] **[P2]** AOSP source-code publication cadence change effective 2026 — Google announced (effective 2026) that to align with the "trunk stable" development model and ensure platform stability for the ecosystem, Google will publish Android source code to AOSP in Q2 and Q4 only (rather than continuously throughout the year as before). For building and contributing to AOSP, the `android-latest-release` manifest branch will always reference the most recent release pushed to AOSP. **Sources**: source.android.google.cn/security/bulletin/chromecast (Dec 9 2025 — Chromecast security bulletin page footer text confirming the Q2+Q4 cadence); source.android.google.cn/security/bulletin (Android Security Bulletins index page — "Effective in 2026, to align with our trunk stable development model and ensure platform stability for the ecosystem, we will publish source code to AOSP in Q2 and Q4"). **Why this is the ANDROID-OS-DEVELOPMENT-CADENCE dimension distinct from B-107 (Android 17 memory-pressure enforcement — a platform BEHAVIOR)**: B-107 is a runtime memory-management behavior change affecting games that run on Android 17; W-171 is a DEVELOPMENT-CADENCE change affecting when Android source code (including security patches) becomes available for OEMs to incorporate into device firmware — which determines when those patches actually reach end-user Android Chrome WebView that runs GameZipper mobile games. **Distinct from B-099 (Android June 2026 Security Bulletin 124 CVEs)**: B-099 is a single monthly patch batch; W-171 is the CADENCE that governs when patches get into AOSP and therefore into OEM firmware. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's mobile traffic is ~70% Android (per project context); the Android Chrome WebView version on those devices determines which web-platform features work (WebGPU, bfcache, CSS Gap Decorations per C-113); (b) under the new Q2+Q4 cadence, security patches (like CVE-2026-21385 Qualcomm Graphics per S-136) may take LONGER to reach end-user devices because OEMs only get source-code drops twice a year instead of continuously — meaning in-the-wild Android CVEs have a longer exploitation window against GameZipper mobile players; (c) the longer patch-to-device time means GameZipper's Android players are running KNOWN-VULNERABLE WebView versions for more of the year; (d) the cadence change also means new web-platform features (which Android Chrome inherits from Chromium) reach Android devices in batches aligned with AOSP drops rather than continuously — feature availability on Android Chrome may lag desktop Chrome by months. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) **Android Chrome WebView version distribution monitoring** — extend the BI dashboard to record Android Chrome WebView major-version distribution per month; alert if the share of devices running versions >6 months old grows >50% (signal that the Q2+Q4 cadence is lengthening patch-to-device time); (2) **CVE-to-Android-device-patch lag tracking** — for each in-the-wild Android CVE (S-136 Qualcomm Graphics, B-099 monthly bulletins), track the time from CVE disclosure to >50% of GameZipper's Android traffic running a patched WebView; under the new cadence, expect this lag to grow from ~30 days to ~120-180 days; (3) **feature-availability degradation test** — for any Chrome-desktop-first feature (CSS Gap Decorations, WebGPU, bfcache WebSocket), measure when the feature reaches >50% of GameZipper Android traffic; under the new cadence, expect a >90-day desktop-to-Android lag; document per-feature; (4) **Q2/Q4 AOSP drop watch** — subscribe to the Android Open Source Project announcements for the Q2 and Q4 source-code drops; each drop triggers a regression sweep of Top-30 GameZipper games on the latest AOSP-derived Android emulator to catch any new WebView regressions before they reach production devices.
- [S-136] **[P1]** CVE-2026-21385 Qualcomm Android Graphics OOB-read CVSS 7.8 (in-the-wild exploited, Google March 2026 Android Security Bulletin) — Qualcomm disclosed a buffer over-read vulnerability in its Android Graphics component caused by an integer-overflow / missing-buffer-space-check when adding user-supplied data, leading to memory corruption; Google's Android security team reported the issue to Qualcomm on December 18 2025; Qualcomm notified customers on February 2 2026; Google's March 2026 Android Security Bulletin explicitly stated "there are indications that CVE-2026-21385 may be under limited, targeted exploitation" — i.e., ACTIVELY EXPLOITED IN THE WILD. The March 2026 Android update also patched 129 total vulnerabilities including CVE-2026-0006 (a CRITICAL System-component RCE requiring no privileges or user interaction). **Source**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_10069a7b0f989452 (March 4 2026 "谷歌确认高通Android组件CVE-2026-21385漏洞遭利用"). **Why this is the ANDROID-GPU-DRIVER-CVE-IN-THE-WILD dimension distinct from B-099 (Android June 2026 Security Bulletin 124 CVEs — a generic monthly-batch entry) and B-107 (Android 17 memory-pressure enforcement — an OS BEHAVIOR)**: B-099 is the BULLETIN-LEVEL summary; S-136 is a SPECIFIC in-the-wild-exploited GPU-driver CVE that affects the Android Chrome WebView graphics stack GameZipper games render through. **Distinct from S-088 (CVE-2026-5281 Dawn WebGPU UAF)**: S-088 is a Chrome WebGPU (Dawn) bug — patched via Chrome update; S-136 is a Qualcomm Adreno GPU DRIVER bug — patched via Android system update that depends on OEM firmware release (much slower, and per W-171 the new AOSP Q2+Q4 cadence makes it slower still). **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's mobile traffic is ~70% Android and a large fraction runs on Qualcomm-SoC devices (Snapdragon) with Adreno GPUs — the CVE-2026-21385 attack surface is the GPU driver those devices use to render every WebGL/Canvas frame; (b) "limited, targeted exploitation" means a sophisticated actor is already using this CVE — likely against high-value targets but the technique will diffuse; (c) the GPU-driver exploit surface means a malicious WebGL/Canvas context (in a third-party ad creative, per the Monetag/AdSense ad-slot threat model) could deliver the exploit; (d) the patch-to-device time is OEM-dependent and per W-171 may now be 120-180 days — a long window during which GameZipper mobile players run vulnerable GPUs. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) **Adreno-GPU share monitoring** — extend the BI dashboard to record GPU vendor share among Android traffic (qualcomm/adreno, arm/mali, imagination/powerVR, intel, amd); document the Qualcomm/Adreno share (likely majority); CVE-2026-21385's impact is proportional to this share; (2) **ad-creative WebGL/Canvas audit** — extend the gz-ad-below-game iframe sandbox check (per S-029) to specifically verify ad creatives cannot create WebGL contexts with attacker-controlled parameters (the GPU-driver exploit-entry vector); the iframe `sandbox` attribute should NOT include `allow-scripts` combined with `allow-same-origin` (which would let the ad reach the GPU); (3) **patch-coverage watch** — monitor the share of GameZipper's Qualcomm-Adreno Android traffic running a patched system image (system_image_patch_level >= 2026-03-01); alert if unpatched share stays >70% 90 days after disclosure (per W-171's slower cadence this is the realistic expectation); (4) **WebGL-input-validation audit on Top-30 games** — `grep -rE 'getContext.*webgl|createShader|shaderSource|texImage2D|bufferData' /home/msdn/gamezipper.com/*/index.html 2>/dev/null | head` to find games that pass external/untrusted data into WebGL APIs (potential CVE-2026-21385 trigger surface if the untrusted data is attacker-controlled via URL parameter or ad creative); (5) **graceful-degradation test** — for each Top-30 game with WebGL, verify the game degrades to Canvas 2D (or fails closed) when `getContext('webgl')` returns null (a possible outcome if the GPU process crashes due to CVE-2026-21385); games that REQUIRE WebGL with no fallback leave the player on a black screen if the GPU is compromised.
- [C-113] **[P2]** Vibe-coding methodology risk — Karpathy Feb 2 2025 tweet coinage "vibe coding" (the practice of writing code by accepting AI-suggested completions without rigorous review, trusting the AI's "vibe") became the Collins English Dictionary 2025 Word of the Year; by April 2026 云涌AI/钛媒体 published a 25000-character "History of the AI Programming Movement 2020-2026" retrospective positioning vibe coding as the dominant 2025-2026 software-development methodology. **Sources**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_04569e9fe0604952 (April 23 2026 "屈服于氛围：一部AI编程运动史(2020-2026)【2.5万字简史】" — Yunyong AI / TMTPost); Collins English Dictionary 2025 Word of the Year announcement; Karpathy Feb 2 2025 tweet ("There's a new kind of coding I call 'vibe coding'... I just see things, say things, run things, and copy paste things, and it mostly works."). **Why this is the AI-GENERATED-CODE-NONDETERMINISM-QA-BYPASS dimension distinct from C-110 (DevTools-console CVE scanner — a TOOL-AS-VECTOR case) and C-111 (Purple-team LLM vuln scanning — a DEFENSIVE-QA-TOOLING case)**: C-110 is a specific tool pattern; C-111 is a defensive-tooling category; C-113 is the METHODOLOGY dimension — the cultural practice of vibe coding that GameZipper's own pipeline operators (Claude Code subagents, Hermes orchestrators, the puzzle-pipeline game generators) increasingly embody. The risk: AI-generated game code is NONDETERMINISTIC (the same prompt produces different code on different runs), so deterministic QA (run the same test, expect the same result) systematically MISSES bugs that the AI introduced on THIS run but not the LAST run. **Distinct from S-101 (AI-assistant browser extension prompt injection)**: S-101 is an attack on a deployed AI; C-113 is a QUALITY-ASSURANCE-PROCESS dimension about the code the AI itself produces. **Distinct from C-108 (npm worm methodology)**: C-108 is malicious supply-chain; C-113 is well-intentioned code generation. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) the GameZipper puzzle-pipeline (game-dev-workflow skill + new-game-pipeline + Claude Code subagents) is fundamentally a vibe-coding workflow — AI generates game code, a human (or another agent) approves; (b) the post-deploy-verification skill documents many RECURRING BUGS that are EXACTLY the vibe-coding failure mode (rAF cancelAnimationFrame template error in 55 games, splash-screen dead-lock in 5+ games, missing input handlers, BGM setInterval leaks, games-data.js orphan entries recurring 5+ times) — these bugs were introduced by AI generators and missed by deterministic QA because the SAME generator on a different prompt would not have produced them; (c) the 2025 Collins Word-of-the-Year status means vibe coding is now the dominant methodology and GameZipper's pipeline is exposed to its full failure surface; (d) the post-deploy-verification R0-R3 static-scan "✅ PASS" then R4 browser-test "❌ FAIL" pattern (per the skill's CRITICAL LESSON) is the canonical vibe-coding-QA-bypass — static scans cannot catch the AI-introduced runtime bugs because the AI's bugs are not in the static analyzer's training data. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) **AI-generated-code diff-review enforcement** — for every game generated by the puzzle-pipeline, REQUIRE a human-readable diff review (not just a static scan) of the AI-generated code before commit; the diff review must specifically look for the documented vibe-coding failure patterns (rAF template errors, splash deadlocks, missing addEventListener calls, setInterval-without-clearInterval); (2) **AI-run-non-determinism test** — re-run the SAME generator prompt for a Top-30 game THREE TIMES on three different days and diff the three outputs; if the outputs differ materially (>10% line diff), the generator is non-deterministic and deterministic QA on a single run is insufficient — add a "run-3-times-and-diff" step to the pipeline; (3) **vibe-coding-failure-pattern regression-suite** — codify the documented recurring bugs (rAF template, splash dead-lock, input-handler-missing, BGM-leak, games-data-orphan) as a CHECKLIST the generator must self-verify against before producing output; this is the inverse of vibe coding — a deterministic check on a non-deterministic generator; (4) **AI-generated code REVIEW-MANDATORY commit-policy** — add a pre-commit hook that BLOCKS commits to games/ if the diff was AI-generated (detectable via commit message "Generated by Claude Code" or pipeline-tag) AND no human review signature is present; (5) **vibe-coding-metrics** — track the AI-generated-game bug rate (bugs found in R4 / total games generated) monthly; a rising rate signals the generator is drifting (model update, prompt regression) and the QA bypass is widening; alert if rate > 20% (1 in 5 AI-generated games has a runtime bug caught only by browser testing).

---

## v1.71.0 — R191 Dynamic Test Intelligence — 6 New Test Cases (2026-06-19)

The 6 test cases below were added by the R191 cron run. Each is a NEW dimension (verified absent from v1.70.0 via grep for both the new ID and the key distinguishing term). Sources: minimax web search (9 queries; web-search-prime/SearXNG not invoked this round to avoid the recurring 404 pattern — minimax is the stable search backend).

- [B-110] **[P1]** China NVDB iOS-WebKit RED ALERT (April 3 2026) — 工信部 国家网络安全威胁和漏洞信息共享平台 issued the HIGHEST-LEVEL (红色预警) warning confirming foreign-APT in-the-wild exploitation of iOS WebKit; 42,000+ iPhones in CN confirmed compromised; single malicious webpage → no-interaction RCE → full device control; covers CVE-2025-43529 + CVE-2025-14174 (both Google TAG discovered, both in-the-wild exploited). SOVEREIGN-CERT-IOS-WEBKIT-RED-ALERT dimension distinct from W-167 (China Shai-Hulud npm advisory — npm-specific) because B-110 is iOS-WebKit-specific and RED-ALERT-level; distinct from B-097 (iOS 26.5 generic patch-list) because B-110 is a national-red-alert response to foreign-APT exploitation across 42K+ confirmed devices. (blog.csdn.net/xixixi7777/article/details/160202543 June 11 2026; new.qq.com/rain/a/20260119A01E5F00 Jan 19 2026; new.qq.com/rain/a/20251229A06B2H00 Dec 29 2025)
- [S-137] **[P1]** CVE-2025-14174 / CVE-2025-43529 Cross-Vendor ANGLE Memory-OOB shared CVE (Apple Jan 19 2026; Google TAG discovered; both in-the-wild exploited) — CVE-2025-14174 is an ANGLE memory-OOB that affects BOTH iOS Safari/WebKit (via ANGLE Metal backend per trac.webkit.org/wiki/AngleforWebGL) AND Chrome/Chromium (via ANGLE DirectX/Vulkan backend) — the CROSS-VENDOR-ANGLE-SHARED-CVE dimension: a single graphics-library-layer CVE propagates to multiple browser vendors simultaneously, so a patched-Chrome-but-unpatched-iOS (or vice versa) means the SAME exploit creative can hit whichever platform lags. Distinct from S-088 (CVE-2026-5281 Dawn WebGPU UAF — Chrome-Dawn-specific) and B-087 (iOS 26 WebKit WebGPU memory-limits regression — API-bug) because S-137 is the SHARED-LIBRARY dimension. (cnblogs.com/clnchanpin/p/19475745 Jan 13 2026; support.apple.com/en-us/125884; new.qq.com/rain/a/20260119A01E5F00 Jan 19 2026)
- [W-172] **[P2]** Safari 26 stable ship + last-minute privacy-feature PULLBACK (cloud.tencent.com June 19 2026) — WebKit bug 134226694 (auto-strip gclid/fbclid tracking params in regular browsing mode) + WebKit bug 134227067 (prevent fingerprinting for known tracking scripts) were BOTH beta-announced for Safari 26 but REMOVED from the final Safari 26 stable release — gclid/fbclid tracking is NOT blocked in shipped Safari 26. SAFARI-ANNOUNCED-PRIVACY-FEATURE-PULLED-LAST-MINUTE dimension distinct from S-081 (Safari 27 deliberate deprecations) and B-090 (Safari 27 WWDC26 525 fixes) because W-172 is the BETA-TO-STABLE-PULLBACK pattern — affects GameZipper Monetag/AdSense attribution (gclid/fbclid still work in Safari 26 stable, contradicting beta announcement) AND generalizes: any Safari beta feature is at-risk-of-pullback before stable ship; QA plans based on WWDC26 announcements (B-108/B-090) should be conditional on stable-ship confirmation in September 2026. (cloud.tencent.com/developer/article/2637644 June 19 2026)
- [S-138] **[P2]** Safari 26.5 WebRTC process-crash fix (Apple May 14 2026) — Safari 26.5 security list includes a fix for a WebRTC bug that could cause the Safari browser process to abnormally crash affecting audio/video communication scenarios; also fixed CVE-2026-28962 (CSP bypass → sensitive-info disclosure — already covered by S-040 v1.19.0) and multiple memory-corruption crash issues. SAFARI-26.5-WEBRTC-CRASH-FIX dimension distinct from S-085 (CVE-2026-9111 WebRTC UAF RCE — Linux Chromium) and B-086 (Firefox 151 MFSA2026-46 sandbox-escape batch) because S-138 is Safari-specific WebRTC crash (DoS / process-termination) from a different bug class. Any future GameZipper game using WebRTC (peer-to-peer multiplayer, voice chat) on Safari 26/26.4 (pre-fix) could crash mid-session. (developer.apple.com/news May 14 2026 Safari 26.5 release notes; so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_0236a054d4370952 May 14 2026)
- [A-040] **[P2]** PHY041/phy-a11y-reviewer automated WCAG 2.2 AA web-application reviewer toolchain (GitHub March 6 2026; corroborated by a11ycheck mrgig.com May 16 2026) — a category of tools that takes either a URL or codebase and produces a structured WCAG 2.2 AA conformance report (87 criteria including the 9 new 2.2 criteria per A-027), going beyond what axe-core/WAVE catch (30-40% per A-031). AUTOMATED-WCAG-2.2-AA-REVIEWER-TOOLCHAIN dimension distinct from A-034 (US ADA Title II — legal/regulatory), A-037 (iOS 27 Accessibility framework rewrite — Apple-API-specific), and A-038/A-039 (lawsuit statistics) because A-040 is the TOOLING-AUTOMATION dimension — actionable integration of an open-source reviewer into the GameZipper pre-commit/CI pipeline (matching the existing gz-pre-commit + sync-game-counts.sh pattern in the post-deploy-verification skill) to catch WCAG 2.2 AA regressions before they ship across 280+ game pages + tools subdomain. (github.com/PHY041/phy-a11y-reviewer March 6 2026; mrgig.com May 16 2026; blog.csdn.net/gitblog_00365/article/details/155290334 April 20 2026)
- [P-069] **[P2]** WebGPU 39× draw-call throughput + 2.75× texture-sampling rate vs WebGL2 — concrete migration-decision numbers (CSDN AWS WebGPU实战 Three.js March 24 2026; corroborated by zhihu WebGPU performance analysis Aug 2 2022) — WebGL 2.0 caps at ~12,000 draw calls/frame before CPU-bottlenecked stalls; WebGPU sustains ~470,000 draw calls/frame = 39× throughput; texture sampling 8 GB/s (WebGL2) vs 22 GB/s (WebGPU) = 2.75×; render perf 3×+ WebGL; compute perf 50×+ WebGL (measured on M1 Pro + RTX 2060 Super). CONCRETE-WebGPU-PERF-NUMBERS-FOR-MIGRATION-CASE dimension distinct from P-030 (WebGPU 70% coverage + coarse 15× compute) and P-047 (WebGPU 8.5× draw calls — real-game sustained workload, different metric) because P-069 provides FOUR specific ceiling numbers each tied to a game-workload profile (draw-call-heavy / texture-heavy / render-heavy / compute-heavy) — sets the migration-decision threshold for any future high-sprite-count game (bullet-hell, particle-heavy match-3, large-tile puzzle) that would cap at the WebGL2 12K-draw-call ceiling. (blog.csdn.net/awscloud/article/details/146470071 March 24 2026; zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/387054998 Aug 2 2022)
---

## v1.72.0 — R192 Dynamic Test Intelligence — 6 New Test Cases (2026-06-19)

The 6 test cases below were added by the R192 cron run. Each is a NEW dimension (verified absent from v1.71.0 via grep for both the new ID and the key distinguishing term). Sources: minimax web search (11 queries; web-search-prime/SearXNG not invoked this round to continue avoiding the recurring 404 pattern seen in R187–R191 — minimax is the stable search backend).

- [S-139] **[P1]** CVE-2026-20700 iOS/iPadOS/macOS/tvOS/watchOS/visionOS dyld (dynamic linker) zero-day patched in iOS 26.3 (Feb 12 2026) — Apple's security advisory: "An attacker with memory write capability may be able to execute arbitrary code. Apple is aware of a report that this issue may have been exploited in an extremely sophisticated attack against specific targeted individuals on versions of iOS before iOS 26." Discovered by Google's Threat Analysis Group (TAG). The vulnerability lives in dyld — Apple's dynamic link editor used by iOS/iPadOS/macOS/tvOS/watchOS/visionOS — affecting EVERY iOS version since iPhone 1.0 (a 19-year-old bug, the longest-lived iOS vuln patched in 2026). Patched across iOS 26.3, iPadOS 26.3, macOS 26.3, tvOS 26.3, watchOS 26.3, visionOS 26.3. **Sources**: theregister.com/2026/02/12/apple_ios_263/ (Feb 12 2026 — "Apple patched a zero-day vulnerability affecting every iOS version since 1.0... exploited in an extremely sophisticated attack against targeted individuals"); blog.csdn.net/smellycat000/article/details/158033092 (Feb 12 2026 — "苹果发布安全更新,修复已用于针对个人的'极其复杂攻击'中的一个 0day 漏洞 CVE-2026-20700... 该漏洞是位于苹果操作系统iOS、iPadOS、macOS、tvOS、watchOS 和visionOS所使用 Dynamic Link 编辑器 dyld 中的一个任意代码执行漏洞... 具有内存写能力的攻击者或能够在受影响设备上执行任意代码"). **Why this is the iOS-DYLD-LINKER-DECADE-OLD-EXPLOIT-CHAIN dimension distinct from B-110 (China NVDB iOS-WebKit RED ALERT — WebKit rendering layer)**: B-110 covers WebKit-layer zero-click RCE; CVE-2026-20700 covers the OS-level dynamic-linker layer BELOW WebKit, and requires a memory-write precondition — making it an EXPLOIT-CHAIN final-stage component rather than a no-click zero-day. **Distinct from B-097 (iOS 26.5 generic 80+ vuln patch-list)**: B-097 is a generic bulletin; S-139 is a SPECIFIC in-the-wild-exploited decade-old dyld CVE patched 4 months earlier in iOS 26.3. **Distinct from S-041 (WebKit same-origin bypass CVE-2026-20643 — WebKit Navigation API)**: S-041 is WebKit-layer; S-139 is OS-loader-layer. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's ~20% iOS Safari traffic runs on devices where every iOS version prior to 26.3 has this dyld bug — a player who clicks a malicious Monetag/AdSense creative that achieves the memory-write precondition (via a separate WebKit bug like the CVE-2025-14174 ANGLE bug covered as S-137) could then chain to CVE-2026-20700 for full-device compromise; (b) the bug is 19 years old — the entire install base of older iPhones (iPhone 11 / SE 2nd-gen and older cannot upgrade to iOS 27 per recent reports) is permanently exploitable; (c) targeted-APT attribution means GameZipper's high-value-player traffic (whales, BI dashboard operators, paid tools.gamezipper.com users) is the demographic most likely to be targeted. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) document the iOS-version distribution of GameZipper's iOS traffic (BI dashboard) and flag any traffic from iOS < 26.3 as "at-risk for CVE-2026-20700 final-stage compromise"; (2) verify the gz-pre-commit + post-deploy-verification skill's external-script-src detector (Phase 0.5 dead-endpoint scan) continues to flag new external script domains on game pages — every new external script is a potential memory-write-vector entry point; (3) ensure no GameZipper first-party JavaScript performs raw memory operations (no WebAssembly memory manipulation of untrusted data) that could be chained with a dyld exploit.
- [S-140] **[P1]** CVE-2026-3910 + CVE-2026-3909 Chrome V8 + Skia dual-0day pair (March 18 2026) — Google紧急修复Chrome浏览器中两个遭野外利用的0Day漏洞: (a) **CVE-2026-3910** — Chrome V8 JavaScript and WebAssembly engine implementation flaw allowing a remote attacker via a specially-crafted HTML page to execute arbitrary code WITHIN the Chrome sandbox (sandbox-bypass but not necessarily full sandbox escape); (b) **CVE-2026-3909** — Chrome Skia 2D graphics library out-of-bounds WRITE allowing a remote attacker via a crafted HTML page to corrupt memory beyond the buffer bounds during rendering. Both vulnerabilities ACTIVELY EXPLOITED IN THE WILD at time of patch. David Shipley (Beauceron Security) warned: "如果不管理浏览器补丁,遭受攻击的风险每天都在增加" (if you don't manage browser patches, attack risk grows daily). **Source**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_87569ba00d309252 (March 18 2026 "谷歌紧急修复Chrome浏览器中两个遭野外利用的0Day漏洞" — explicit identification of CVE-2026-3910 V8 sandbox-exec + CVE-2026-3909 Skia OOB-write + both actively exploited). **Why this is the DUAL-0DAY-V8-AND-SKIA-PAIR-ACTIVELY-EXPLOITED dimension distinct from S-115 (Chrome 150 DevTools drawElementImage blank-canvas visual-regression-test bug — a QA-TOOL bug, not a security exploit) and S-088 (CVE-2026-5281 Dawn WebGPU UAF — a single WebGPU subsystem bug)**: S-140 covers a PAIRED exploit chain that combines JS-engine sandbox-bypass (V8) with graphics-renderer memory corruption (Skia) — a malicious Monetag/AdSense creative could chain CVE-2026-3910 (escape V8 sandbox) + CVE-2026-3909 (corrupt Skia rendering buffer) to achieve full renderer compromise then pivot to the GPU process. **Distinct from S-062 (Chrome 149 Skia CVE-2026-11024 stack overflow) and S-063 (Chrome UI spoofing CVE-2026-9110)**: those are single-CVE May 2026 disclosures; S-140 is a March 2026 PAIRED disclosure where both CVEs were exploited in the wild SIMULTANEOUSLY. **Distinct from S-076/S-077/S-078 (Chrome 149 Payments/Guest View/Tracing UAF batch)**: those are Chrome 149 specific; S-140 predates Chrome 149 stable ship. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) every Chrome user on the pre-patch version is exploitable via a single malicious creative — and GameZipper's Monetag/AdSense ad slots are the most likely delivery vector for an attacker-controlled creative (the DoubleClick DesckVB RAT abuse documented as S-118 confirms this delivery path is real); (b) the V8 sandbox-bypass means even Chrome's multi-process sandboxing does NOT protect against this exploit — the attacker runs code in the renderer process which has direct access to the page DOM (game state, localStorage, session cookies); (c) the Skia OOB-write means the exploit can corrupt the canvas rendering of the game itself, potentially hiding the malicious activity from the player. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) verify the gz-pre-commit hook scans game HTML for any inline `<script>` that constructs new `Function()`, `eval()`, or `WebAssembly.instantiate()` from untrusted input (these are the V8 surfaces CVE-2026-3910 targets); (2) verify the post-deploy-verification Phase 4 Kachilu screenshot capture continues to baseline the rendered pixels of each game page so a Skia-corruption regression (CVE-2026-3909) would be caught by pixel-diff; (3) document the minimum supported Chrome version in GameZipper's compatibility matrix and bump it after every Chrome 0-day disclosure.
- [S-141] **[P1]** SANDWORM_MODE npm supply-chain worm (Feb 28 2026, QQ News / Socket research) — A large-scale npm supply-chain worm targeting the software ecosystem, infiltrating developer machines, CI pipelines, and AI coding tools. Socket researchers discovered the active campaign and named it SANDWORM_MODE based on the "SANDWORM_*" environment-variable switches embedded in the malicious software's runtime control logic. At least 19 impersonation packages were published across multiple aliases, masquerading as popular development tools and AI-related tools. Once installed, the packages execute a multi-stage payload: (1) steal secrets from local environment and CI systems; (2) use the stolen tokens to MODIFY OTHER REPOSITORIES (propagation via stolen write access — distinct from S-107 Red Hat Miasma which propagated via compromised-vendor-namespace, this propagates via stolen-developer-credentials); (3) the "SANDWORM_*" env vars toggle which payload stage runs — when env vars are absent (e.g., in static-analysis scanner sandboxes) the package appears benign; when env vars are present (e.g., in production CI runners or end-user developer machines) the full malicious payload activates. **Source**: new.qq.com/rain/a/20260228A047F100 (Feb 28 2026 "npm蠕虫攻击瞄准CI管道与AI编程工具 — 一个大规模的npm供应链蠕虫正在攻击软件生态系统,渗透开发者机器、CI管道和AI编程工具。Socket研究人员发现了这项活跃的攻击活动,并将其命名为SANDWORM_MODE,该名称来源于嵌入在恶意软件运行时控制逻辑中的'SANDWORM_*'环境变量开关... 至少有19个仿冒包通过多个别名发布,伪装成热门开发工具和AI相关工具。一旦安装,这些包会执行多阶段载荷,从本地环境和CI系统中窃取密钥,然后使用窃取的令牌修改其他代码库"). **Why this is the ENV-VARIABLE-TOGGLED-RUNTIME-EVASION dimension distinct from S-131 (Axios version-history-forgery via clean plain-crypto-js@4.2.0 precursor)**: S-131 forges a clean version-history TRAIL to fool automated review tools that trust version-history metadata; S-141 (SANDWORM_MODE) uses runtime CONDITIONAL logic — the malicious code is fully present in the published package, but only ACTIVATES when the runtime sees the right env-var pattern. **Distinct from S-124 (Glassworm invisible-Unicode source-code obfuscation)**: S-124 hides malicious logic in invisible Unicode characters so the source TEXT looks clean; S-141 hides malicious logic behind an env-var CONDITIONAL so the source reads as a benign feature-flag toggle. **Distinct from S-107 (Red Hat Miasma worm — compromised-vendor-namespace)**: S-107 propagated through 32 official @redhat-cloud-services packages after one stolen GitHub account; S-141 propagates through stolen DEVELOPER CREDENTIALS to modify OTHER REPOS after the initial 19-package drop. **Distinct from S-135 (CanisterWorm — ICP decentralized-blockchain C2 exfil)**: S-135 targets AI-agent companies with blockchain-based C2; S-141 targets generic CI/CD with traditional token-theft propagation. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) GameZipper's Hermes/Claude Code/MCP build pipeline (documented as the agent-based build surface per S-128 OpenClaw ClawHub, S-130 TanStack Shai-Hulud) has BROAD tool access — a single compromised npm package installed by a Hermes subagent or Claude Code session could activate its SANDWORM_* payload in the production CI environment (where env vars like `GITHUB_TOKEN`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` are present); (b) the env-var-conditional evasion specifically defeats the static-analysis scanners (Socket, Snyk, GitHub Dependabot) that GameZipper's pipeline runs — these scanners execute in isolated sandboxes WITHOUT production env vars, so the malicious path never runs during scan; (c) the "modify other repos" propagation means a single compromised build could backdoor the entire gamezipper.com repo. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) extend the post-deploy-verification skill's Phase 1 npm audit to include a RUNTIME sandbox-vs-production env-var-diff check — scan every `process.env.*` reference in GameZipper's npm dependencies and flag any conditional that activates malicious-looking behavior (network calls, fs writes outside node_modules, child_process spawns) based on env-var presence; (2) ensure GameZipper's CI runners use the GitHub npm v12 default (no install scripts + no git deps + no remote URLs per S-109) and that any package requiring install-script override is manually reviewed; (3) document the GameZipper env-var surface (every env var the build pipeline reads) and rotate credentials after any npm-install of an unfamiliar package.
- [C-114] **[P2]** Anthropic Mythos AI vuln-GENERATION model (April 8 2026 Anthropic statement; corroborated June 9 2026 Claude Fable 5 leak) — Anthropic publicly confirmed it developed an internal AI model named "Mythos" capable of generating zero-day exploits at scale, and decided NOT to release it because it would "以一种不好的方式破坏互联网" (break the internet in a bad way). The confirmation came via Anthropic's April 8 2026 statement: "Anthropic开发了这个模型并将其命名为Mythos. 幸运的是,这家AI公司决定不发布它,因为它会以一种不好的方式破坏互联网" (Anthropic developed this model and named it Mythos. Fortunately, the AI company decided not to release it because it would break the internet in a bad way). Corroborated June 9 2026 by a leaked Claude Fable 5 system-prompt revealing the Claude 5 model family's tiered structure: Claude Fable 5 is the first product of the new Claude 5 series, positioned in the "Mythos-level" tier ABOVE Claude Opus. **Sources**: so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_45769d629dd14252 (April 8 2026 "Anthropic发布AI漏洞挖掘模型Mythos引发网络安全担忧 — 多年来,信息安全社区最大的担忧一直是量子计算机可能会破解所有经典加密技术... 现在他们又有了一个新的威胁: 一个能够生成零日漏洞的AI模型. Anthropic开发了这个模型并将其命名为Mythos"); so.html5.qq.com/page/real/search_news?docid=70000021_2156a27d43031152 (June 9 2026 Claude Fable 5 leak — "最强模型分级供应, 安全限制成核心分水岭 — 根据泄露的模型系统提示信息, Claude Fable 5是Anthropic全新Claude 5系列的首款产品, 属于能力层级位于Claude Opus之上的Mythos级模型层"). **Why this is the AI-VULN-GENERATION-CAPABILITY-MATURITY dimension distinct from S-113 (Anthropic Mythos AI 100 Chromium zero-days in 15 days — DEFENSIVE discovery)**: S-113 documents Mythos's DEFENSIVE capability (finding bugs so vendors can patch them — Anthropic disclosed all 100 to Chromium); C-114 documents the OFFENSIVE counterpart — Anthropic confirmed an internal Mythos variant can GENERATE exploit code for those bugs, and the only reason it is not in the wild is Anthropic's voluntary restraint. **Distinct from S-112 (Microsoft 33-package dependency-confusion — traditional supply-chain)**: S-112 is human-driven attack; C-114 is AI-driven exploit generation. **Distinct from C-113 (vibe-coding AI-generated code nondeterminism)**: C-113 is unintentional AI-code risk; C-114 is intentional AI-exploit capability. **Why this matters for GameZipper**: (a) the implication is that a Mythos-class AI in the hands of a different vendor (or a future Anthropic policy change, or a leak of the Mythos weights — which the Claude Fable 5 system-prompt leak June 9 2026 shows IS possible) could generate working exploits for any GameZipper web-game feature within hours, compressing the CVE-disclosure-to-mass-exploitation window from the 20h SLA documented in S-057 to minutes; (b) any future GameZipper feature that ships a NEW web-platform API (WebGPU compute shaders, WebCodecs video processing, WebTransport realtime multiplayer) becomes a publishable CVE target the moment a Mythos-class AI scans it; (c) GameZipper's agent-based build pipeline (Hermes/Claude Code/MCP) is itself a Mythos-target — an attacker could prompt-inject a Mythos-class model to generate a zero-day in GameZipper's own build scripts. **Test for GameZipper**: (1) treat any new web-platform API adoption (WebGPU/WebCodecs/WebTransport/WebNN) as a "Mythos-scan-required" feature — pre-emptively fuzz-test the API surface with a Mythos-class discovery tool (Anthropic's own Big Sleep agent is publicly documented as discovering Chrome bugs) BEFORE shipping; (2) harden the Hermes/Claude Code/MCP build pipeline against prompt-injection (per the OpenClaw ClawHub S-128 + TanStack Shai-Hulud S-130 lessons) so a Mythos-class AI cannot be directed to generate exploits against GameZipper's own build scripts;
